Skip to content
Back Matter

Bibliography

Every source cited across the twenty-seven chapters, deduplicated and grouped by source type.

This bibliography pulls together 1436 unique sources cited in Core Marketing. Where the same source carries a citation in more than one chapter, the entry appears once here and lists every chapter that uses it, so a reader looking for the source of a Sharp finding or a Cialdini principle can see which chapters lean on it without paging through each one separately.

The sources fall into four working categories. Books and book chapters carry the canonical marketing literature the course is built on, from Byron Sharp on brand growth and Philip Kotler on the discipline as a whole to Hubbard on the value of information and Wickham on the data-science workflow that underwrites the analytics chapters. Journal articles and conference papers carry the empirical record, where claims about double jeopardy, customer-equity multipliers, or attribution-model accuracy get their underlying numbers. White papers and research reports carry the practitioner research, where firms like Forrester, Nielsen, McKinsey, and the various consulting houses publish the working numbers a marketer needs to set a budget or write a brief. Web sources carry the regulatory, vendor, and journalistic documentation that the discipline now depends on, where the FTC's enforcement letters, an Anthropic pricing page, or a Stratechery essay matters as much as a peer-reviewed paper.

Within each category the entries are alphabetized by the first significant word in the citation, usually the lead author's surname. Each entry ends with chips linking to the chapter or chapters where the source is used, so a reader can jump from the bibliography to the place in the prose where the citation does its work.

Books Journals Reports Web
01 // books and book chapters

1 Books and Book Chapters (713)

  1. About Amazon, “A Refreshed Grocery Shopping Experience Is Coming to Our Amazon Fresh Stores,” April 3, 2024; The Information and Gizmodo, April 2-3, 2024 (the Just Walk Out reversal and the ~1,000 human reviewers in India). Retail Dive, “Amazon Removes Just Walk Out Tech from Amazon Fresh Stores, Replacing with Dash Carts,” April 3, 2024. CSP Daily News through January 27, 2026, on the Amazon Go contraction from the 2023 peak against the 2018 plan. Retail Technology Innovation Hub, May 3, 2026, on Just Walk Out retained as a B2B licensed product. Canonical 2024-2026 Gap 2 case with the post-reversal B2B-pivot arc. Cited in: Ch 9
  2. Acquired Briefing (Kyle Westaway), Trader Joe’s deep-dive on the narrow-assortment, private-label, single-supplier model; Numerator private-label dollar-share data, twelve months ending June 30, 2024 (Aldi 80 percent, Trader Joe’s 69 percent, Costco 34 percent), as compiled in Retail Brew, August 12, 2024; eMarketer, “Trader Joe’s in Expansion Mode,” 2024 (34 openings in 2024 vs. ~11 in 2023; private-label sales growth 3.9 percent vs. 1.0 percent for national brands); Grocery Dive coverage 2024-2025 (579 stores year-end 2024 across 42 states plus D.C.; Placer.ai foot traffic +9 percent March 2025 YoY); Morning Consult, “Who Shops at Trader Joe’s,” April 2024 (25 percent of frequent shoppers earn $100,000-plus); Expanded Ramblings, “Trader Joe’s Statistics,” 2025 (estimated 2024-2025 revenue $24-25B; 2025 ACSI rank one among U.S. supermarkets).   33. Andrej Karpathy, “Sequoia Ascent 2026 summary,” karpathy.bearblog.dev, April 30, 2026, karpathy.bearblog.dev/sequoia-ascent-2026 . The April 30, 2026 Sequoia AI Ascent keynote that updates the June 17, 2025 YC AI Startup School talk titled “Software Is Changing (Again)” (ref-11, ref-12). Three named concepts the 2026 talk adds to the 2025 framing: the context window as the programming surface a person designs against (where the 2025 talk treated the prompt as the program); agentic engineering as the disciplined practice of building specifications, supervision checkpoints, and structured verifiers around an LLM loop, contrasted with vibe coding as the floor-raising practice that lets a non-technical user ship a working application through plain-English description alone; and the verification rule (“traditional software automates what you can specify; AI automates what you can verify”) as the boundary on where Software 3.0 can be applied. The talk also dates a December 2025 inflection point at which coding agents produced large blocks of working output without per-line correction, corroborated by the Anthropic Economic Index Learning curves report at ref-29. Secondary coverage: Guilherme Flor, “Sequoia AI Ascent 2026: Andrej Karpathy,” The AI Opportunities, April 30, 2026, theaiopportunities.com/p/sequoia-ai-ascent-2026-andrej-karpathy . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-karpathy-sequoia-ascent-2026-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1

Cited in: Ch 27

  68. Anthropic. (2025). Skills: A Standard for AI Workflow Composition. Anthropic Engineering Blog , December 18, 2025. The open-sourced Skill standard. Cited in: Ch 14
  69. Anthropic. (2025, December 18). Agent Skills specification. Beta October 2025; open standard December 18, 2025. Skills work across Claude apps, Claude Code, and the API. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/anthropic-skills-launch-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  70. Anthropic. (2025, September 29). Context management for the Claude Developer Platform: memory tool ( memory_20250818 beta tool type; six commands: view, create, str_replace, insert, delete, rename, addressed against paths beginning with /memories ) and context-editing strategy ( clear_tool_uses_20250919 , beta header context-management-2025-06-27 , configured by trigger / keep / clear_at_least / exclude_tools). Anthropic-reported benchmarks: 39 percent improvement on internal agentic evaluations from the two primitives combined; 29 percent from context editing alone; 84 percent token reduction on a 100-turn web-search workflow with both enabled. Sonnet 4.5 release the same day named a 30-hour autonomous coding-task ceiling and 61.4 percent OSWorld with native context awareness (the model itself reasons about how many tokens remain in its window). claude.com/blog/context-management ; platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/memory-tool ; anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-4-5 . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-anthropic-memory-context-management-2025-2026-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1
  71. Anthropic. (2026). Pricing. Haiku 4.5 $1 / $5 per million tokens; Sonnet 4.6 $3 / $15; Opus 4.7 $5 / $25. Prompt caching cuts cached-input cost by 90%; Batches API cuts both sides by 50%. OpenAI GPT-5 has comparable Nano / GPT-5 / Pro tiers. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-the-model-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  72. Anthropic. Project Vend Phase 1 (March–April 2025, Sonnet 3.7) and Phase 2 (December 18, 2025, Sonnet 4.0/4.5 with CRM, cost-basis inventory, payment-link tool, and a Seymour Cash CEO agent under a 50% minimum-margin rule). Phase 2 measured an ~80% reduction in discounts and a ~50% reduction in free items relative to Phase 1; weeks with negative margin were largely eliminated, with the residual verdict that “the gap between ‘capable’ and ‘completely robust’ remains wide.” anthropic.com/research/project-vend-1 ; anthropic.com/research/project-vend-2 ; red.anthropic.com/2025/project-vend-2 . Project Deal (December 2025): 69 Anthropic employees across four parallel Slack-based classified-ad marketplaces; 186 deals worth just over $4,000. Opus 4.5 sellers extracted $2.68 more per item than Haiku 4.5 sellers (p=0.030); Opus buyers paid $2.45 less per item (p=0.015); Opus users completed 2.07 more deals on average (p=0.001); fairness ratings 4.05 vs 4.06 on a 1–7 scale (statistically indistinguishable); 11 of 28 participants ranked their Haiku run above their Opus run despite measurably worse outcomes. anthropic.com/features/project-deal . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-anthropic-project-vend-deal-2025-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1
  73. Anthropic. “Mapping the Mind of a Large Language Model” (May 21, 2024); “Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models” and “On the Biology of a Large Language Model” (both March 27, 2025); open-source circuit-tracing library released 2025 with the Neuronpedia front end. The 2024 work used sparse-autoencoder dictionary learning on Claude 3.0 Sonnet to extract millions of human-interpretable features; the 2025 work introduced attribution graphs built on a 30-million-feature cross-layer transcoder. Concrete discovered circuits in Claude 3.5 Haiku include the Dallas-to-Texas-to-Austin two-hop reasoning chain, backward planning in poetry, language-agnostic semantic processing, and an entity-recognition gate whose misfires produce a class of hallucinations. transformer-circuits.pub/2025/attribution-graphs/biology.html . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-anthropic-circuit-tracing-2024-2025-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1

Aral and Walker, “Identifying Influential and Susceptible Members,” Science 337 (Jul 2012): 337–341 (1.3M-user trial; r = −0.61); Aral-Walker, “Creating Social Contagion,” Mgmt Sci 57, 9 (2011): 1623–1639 (active 246% / passive 98%); Aral-Walker, “Tie Strength, Embeddedness,” Mgmt Sci 60, 6 (2014): 1352–1370 (tie strength ~60% of influence variance); Bakshy, Eckles, Yan, Rosenn, “Social Influence in Social Advertising,” EC '12 (2012): 146–161 (23M-user peer-endorsement; +50% CTR, +35% conversion, 4× close-friend); Aral-Eckles, “Protecting Elections,” Science 365 (2019): 858–861; Aral, The Hype Machine (Currency, 2020). Cited in: Ch 25

Avinash Kaushik, Web Analytics 2.0 (Sybex/Wiley 2009); DARMA, four-question dashboard audit, macro/micro-conversion, Critical Few KPI, Acquisition-Behavior-Outcomes test, See-Think-Do-Care intent ladder (2013). kaushik.net/avinash .   102. B. Joseph Pine II, Mass Customization (HBS Press, 1993); Pine and Gilmore, “The Four Faces of Mass Customization,” HBR Jan–Feb 1997; Pine and Gilmore, The Experience Economy (HBS Press, 1999). Cited in: Ch 8
  103. B. Sharp 2014, Ch. 12; Sharp 2e, Ch. 11. Old Spice "Smell Like a Man, Man" campaign analyzed as the canonical case of consistent distinctive-asset deployment across varying creative executions. Cited in: Ch 7
  104. B. Sharp, How Brands Grow: What Marketers Don't Know (Oxford University Press, 2010, rev. 2014); B. Sharp ed., Marketing: Theory, Evidence, Practice , 2nd ed. (Oxford University Press, 2017), Chs. 1, 3, 11, 12. Brand-attribution baseline (~40% of viewers correctly linking an ad to its brand) per Franzen 1994 and Rossiter and Bellman 2005; television-advertising wastage (<20% of exposures both noticed and correctly branded) per Sharp 2010, p. 7. Cited in: Ch 7
  105. Bail, Clinton, Dorff, Kenkel, and Larson, “Synthetic Replacements for Human Survey Data? The Perils of Large Language Models,” Political Analysis , 2025. Wiki synthesis: wiki/external/frontier/ai-perils-synthetic-survey-bail-2025-ch05.md . Cited in: Ch 5
  106. Balfour, B. (2017-2026). Reforge essays and curriculum. Reforge.com : “The Four Fits for $100M+ Growth” (2017); “Growth Loops are the New Funnels” (2018); “The Hidden Patterns Behind Successful Growth Programs” (2020). Chen, A. (2021). The Cold Start Problem: How to Start and Scale Network Effects . Harper Business. Growth-engineering vs. growth-PM team-org patterns formalized across the 2018-2024 Reforge growth-series and the a16z Andreessen Horowitz portfolio essays; community-led-growth thesis crystallized in the 2022-2026 Lenny Rachitsky Substack and the CMX (Bevy) State of Community-Led Growth annual report. Last-click attribution failure mode documented in Avinash Kaushik (2010, 2nd ed.), Web Analytics 2.0 , Sybex, and Google’s 2018-2023 data-driven attribution rollouts. Cited in: Ch 23
  107. Barbara E. Kahn, The Shopping Revolution: How Retailers Succeed in an Era of Endless Disruption Accelerated by COVID-19 , 2nd ed. (Wharton School Press, 2021; expanded from the 2018 first edition), wsp.wharton.upenn.edu/book/the-shopping-revolution ; barbarakahn.com/the-shopping-revolution . The canonical Wharton-school reference for the post-2015 retail competitive landscape and the third U.S. retail textbook every elective draws from alongside Berman-Evans (the strategic-process spine, ref-9) and Levy-Weitz (the 6Ps retail mix). The book codifies the Retailing Success Matrix, a two-axis map (product axis: undifferentiated lowest-price to differentiated assortment; experience axis: frictionless to immersive) whose four quadrants name four winning archetypes (the Amazon Bar quadrant of Walmart and Amazon; the lowest-price-experiential quadrant of Costco and Aldi; the differentiated-frictionless quadrant of Warby Parker and the broader DTC cohort; the differentiated-experiential quadrant of Sephora, Apple, REI, and Trader Joe's) and one losing position (the muddled middle of Sears, Toys R Us, RadioShack, Bed Bath and Beyond, JCPenney). Companion: Knowledge at Wharton interview, “The Shopping Revolution: How Retailers Can Survive the Amazon Economy,” knowledge.wharton.upenn.edu/article/the-shopping-revolution-how-retailers-can-survive-the-amazon-economy ; HBR podcast, “The Future of Retail in the Age of Amazon,” October 2018, hbr.org/podcast/2018/10/the-future-of-retail-in-the-age-of-amazon . Kahn faculty page at Wharton: marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/profile/kahn . Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/kahn-shopping-revolution-ch19.md . Cited in: Ch 12
  108. Behavioral-Economics Bias Catalog 2024-2026 (course reader), core-course/wiki/external/frontier/behavioral-econ-bias-catalog-ch04.md . Indexes twenty-eight named buyer biases across six families with effect sizes, 2015-2026 replication strength, and brand applications. The twenty-eight reduce to five mechanisms (memory limitation, reference-dependent valuation, effort minimization, social verification, time inconsistency). Cited in: Ch 4
  109. Berman, Evans, and Chatterjee, Retail Management: A Strategic Approach , 13th ed. (Pearson, 2018), Chapter 3 on the Strategic Retail Management process and the controllable / uncontrollable variable split. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/berman-evans-retail-ch12.md . Cited in: Ch 12
  110. Bernd H. Schmitt, Experiential Marketing: How to Get Customers to Sense, Feel, Think, Act, and Relate to Your Company and Brands (Free Press, 1999); Schmitt, Customer Experience Management (Wiley, 2003). Five strategic experiential modules (SENSE, FEEL, THINK, ACT, RELATE) as the inventory of non-functional axes brands compete on once functional parity holds. Cited in: Ch 4
  111. Bert Rosenbloom, Marketing Channels: A Management View , 8th ed. (Cengage / South-Western, 2013), source of the seven-step channel-design process, the eight flows of distribution (Vaile-Grether-Cox 1952), and the three-part channel-member motivation cycle (find needs, offer support, provide leadership). Cited in: Ch 11

Cited in: Ch 10

  147.   159. Christensen, Hall, Dillon, Duncan, Competing Against Luck (Harper Business, 2016); Christensen et al., “Know Your Customers’ Jobs to Be Done,” HBR , Sept 2016; Anthony W. Ulwick, What Customers Want (McGraw-Hill, 2005). Four Forces of Progress; ODI opportunity-score formula. Cited in: Ch 8
  160. Christine Moorman et al., The CMO Survey , 35th edition (Spring 2025) and 36th edition (Spring 2026), Duke University Fuqua School of Business, Deloitte, and the American Marketing Association; cmosurvey.org/results . Spring 2025 reports marketing budgets at 9.4 percent of revenue and projects 8.9 percent overall budget growth and 11.9 percent digital-budget growth into 2026. Spring 2026 reports the ratio declining to 9.0 percent with growth slowing to 1.7 percent, the highest pessimism reading since 2020, customer retention surpassing acquisition and brand as the strongest performance driver, and training budgets compressing to 3.8 percent of marketing spend. Gartner’s 2025 CMO Spend Survey (May 12, 2025) reports the marketing-to-revenue ratio flatlined at 7.7 percent for a second consecutive year with 59 percent of CMOs reporting insufficient budget; gartner.com/en/newsroom (May 2025) . Cited in: Ch 25
  161. Christopher Lovelock and Jochen Wirtz, Services Marketing: People, Technology, Strategy , 8th ed. (World Scientific, 2016), on service-recovery design, the Tax-Brown-Chandrashekaran 1998 recovery effect, and the canonical 7Ps framework. Also Valarie A. Zeithaml, Mary Jo Bitner, and Dwayne D. Gremler, Services Marketing: Integrating Customer Focus Across the Firm , 7th ed. (McGraw-Hill, 2018), Chapter 10 on blueprinting and Chapter 19 on recovery. Cited in: Ch 9
  162. Cialdini, Influence: The Psychology of Persuasion , rev. ed. (Harper Business, 2006). Anchors include Regan (1971), Freedman and Fraser (1966), Phillips (1974-1986), Hofling et al. (1966), Worchel et al. (1975). Cited in: Ch 4

Rotisserie chicken: 157.4M birds in FY2025, $4.99 retail price held since 2009, with Lincoln Premium Poultry vertical-integration plant (Fremont NE, ~2M birds/week, opened 2019) protecting the price against input-cost drift. Cournot, Recherches sur les principes mathématiques de la théorie des richesses (1838) (quantity-competition model); Bertrand, Journal des Savants 67 (1883): 499-508 (price-competition model and the Bertrand paradox); Tirole, The Theory of Industrial Organization (MIT Press, 1988), Part II Chs 5-7 (modern synthesis: capacity-constrained Bertrand, differentiated-product Bertrand, repeated-game Bertrand); Kreps and Scheinkman, “Quantity Precommitment and Bertrand Competition Yield Cournot Outcomes,” Bell J. Econ. 14 (1983): 326-337. Synthesis at canonical/bertrand-cournot-tirole-ch10.md . Cited in: Ch 10

Cracker Barrel Old Country Store, Inc., 8-K filings, August-September 2025; YouGov BrandIndex tracking data on Cracker Barrel, August 19 to September 12, 2025; CEO Julie Felss Masino public statements, August 25 and September 2, 2025; Wall Street Journal , “Cracker Barrel Walks Back Logo Refresh,” September 3, 2025.   192. CSA Research, 2024 State of the Industry Report (~$74.7B language-services market; AI first-pass adoption ~78% in 2024 vs. ~41% in 2021); Weglot, 2024 State of Localization Marketing , n=1,200 (67% generative-AI first-draft, 82% human transcreation retained); ACL WMT 2024 on LLM-augmented MT matching human-only on FLORES-200 in 38 of 200+ pairs and lagging by 12-18 BLEU on English-Japanese / English-Korean. Synthesis in wiki/external/frontier/ai-translation-localization-2024-2026-ch15.md . Cited in: Ch 15
  193. CStore Dive, “Whole Foods Plans National Rollout for Daily Shop Concept,” 2024 (~9,000 sq ft format vs. ~40,000 sq ft flagship; ~75 percent SKU coverage; fill-in-trip positioning); Grocery Dive, “Whole Foods Daily Shop,” June 2025 (10,000 sq ft Queens commissary supplying daily prepared foods; deliberate placement between existing full-size stores to add occasions without cannibalizing); Whole Foods Market Newsroom, Daily Shop opening press releases for Hoboken (December 2025) and Brooklyn Williamsburg (February 2026). Frontier reader: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/whole-foods-daily-shop-2025-ch12.md . Cited in: Ch 12
  194. Customer data platform and clean-room vendor documentation: Twilio Segment product and pricing pages; mParticle product documentation and 2025 Rokt acquisition coverage; Twilio Customer Engagement Platform release notes; Treasure Data documentation (Arm Treasure Data); AWS Clean Rooms whitepapers and pricing (general availability March 2023); Habu (acquired by LiveRamp January 2024) documentation; InfoSum federated-architecture whitepapers; Snowflake Data Clean Room documentation; LiveRamp RampID, The Trade Desk Unified ID 2.0, and Acxiom AbiliTec identity-graph specifications. Industry coverage of post-cookie ID resolution in AdExchanger , Digiday , and IAB Tech Lab 2023-2026. Cited in: Ch 11
  195. Customer.io. Pricing . Customer.io, 2026. Essentials $100/mo (5K profiles, 1M emails); Premium $1,000/mo (10K profiles); Startup Plan free for 12 months for companies under $10M raised. customer.io/pricing . Cited in: Ch 14
  196. D. A. Aaker and E. Joachimsthaler, Brand Leadership (Free Press, 2000), Ch. 4 (Brand Architecture); Aaker, Brand Portfolio Strategy (Free Press, 2004). The brand relationship spectrum (branded house, sub-brand, endorsed brand, house of brands), with Lexus 1989 and the Marriott portfolio as canonical cases. Canonical readers: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/aaker-brands-ch07.md ; aaker-joachimsthaler-brand-leadership-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  197. D. A. Aaker, Managing Brand Equity (Free Press, 1991); Building Strong Brands (Free Press, 1996). The four-dimensional brand-equity model (awareness, perceived quality, associations, loyalty); operationalized in Yoo & Donthu (2001) and Pappu et al. (2005). Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/aaker-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  198. D. B. Holt and D. Cameron, Cultural Strategy: Using Innovative Ideologies to Build Breakthrough Brands (Oxford University Press, 2010). Six-step operational framework (deconstruct the category's cultural orthodoxy; identify the ideological opportunity opened by macro disruption; scout populist worlds the brand has resident standing in; compose the identity myth as setting, protagonist, and moral; design cultural-prototype tests against insiders; operationalize coherent expression across every touchpoint). Seven-method research toolkit (brand genealogy, category genealogy, cultural-history reconstruction, populist-world ethnography, ideological-mapping, prototype testing, touchpoint audit). Worked cases include Nike (combative solo willpower against the early-1980s athletic-feat orthodoxy, drawn from urban African-American sport subculture), Jack Daniel's (frontier-Tennessee heritage against the luxury-status whisky orthodoxy of the 1990s-2000s), Starbucks, Ben & Jerry's, Patagonia, Mini, Fuse, vitaminwater, Svedka, Freelancers Union, Fat Tire. 2023-2026 worked-case extensions: Liquid Death's 2019-2024 growth ($1.4B valuation, certified-B-Corp, SNL plus TikTok placements running the wellness-as-restraint counter-myth); Bud Light's 2023 Mulvaney campaign (populist-world authenticity-test failure inside the customer base the brand already had; ~30 percent share loss within a year per HBR , March 2024); Patagonia's 2022 transfer of ownership to a climate trust as the cultural-strategy alignment Sharp-Aaker dashboards alone cannot diagnose. Holt earlier work: How Brands Become Icons (HBS Press, 2004) at ref-42. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/holt-cultural-branding-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  199. D. B. Holt, How Brands Become Icons: The Principles of Cultural Branding (Harvard Business School Press, 2004). Cultural-branding model: iconic brands perform identity myths drawn from populist worlds that resolve cultural anxieties of their era (Marlboro, Volkswagen, Harley-Davidson, Nike). Cited in: Ch 7
  200. Damasio, Descartes' Error: Emotion, Reason, and the Human Brain (Putnam, 1994); Bechara, Damasio, Damasio, and Anderson, "Insensitivity to Future Consequences Following Damage to Human Prefrontal Cortex," Cognition , 50(1-3), 1994, 7-15; Bechara and Damasio, "The Somatic Marker Hypothesis: A Neural Theory of Economic Decision," Games and Economic Behavior , 52(2), 2005, 336-372. Iowa Gambling Task and ventromedial prefrontal cortex lesion-patient evidence; neural mechanism beneath System 1 pattern-match. Marketing application via Plassmann, O'Doherty, Shiv, and Rangel, "Marketing Actions Can Modulate Neural Representations of Experienced Pleasantness," PNAS , 105(3), 2008, 1050-1054 (placebo-price wine fMRI replicating the Shiv-Carmon-Ariely 2005 finding at the vmPFC level). Cited in: Ch 4
  201. danah boyd, It's Complicated (Yale, 2014), 166 teen interviews 2003–2012; boyd, “Social Network Sites as Networked Publics,” in Papacharissi (ed.), Networked Self (Routledge, 2010): 39–58, formalizing four affordances (persistence, visibility, spreadability, searchability) and three dynamics (invisible audiences, context collapse, public/private blur). Synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/boyd-its-complicated-ch19.md . Cited in: Ch 19
  202. Daniel Kahneman, Olivier Sibony, and Cass R. Sunstein, Noise: A Flaw in Human Judgment (Little, Brown Spark, 2021). Three noise components (level, stable pattern, occasion) and decision-hygiene practices applied to LLM-mediated buyer funnels in 2026. Cited in: Ch 4
  203. Daniel Kahneman, Thinking, Fast and Slow (FSG, 2011). Dual-system framework underpinning the heuristics-and-biases literature. Cited in: Ch 4
  204. Daniel M. Sherman, Affiliate Program Management: An Hour a Day , 2nd ed. (Sybex, 2022); Performance Marketing Association, 2026 Industry Benchmarks . Cited in: Ch 20
  205. Dario Amodei. (2024, October 11). Machines of Loving Grace. Central definition of powerful AI : a system smarter than a Nobel laureate across most fields, capable of autonomous hours-to-weeks task completion, runnable as millions of instances at 10–100x human speed. Arrival as early as 2026, no later than 2030; compressed-21st-century claim: 50–100 years of biomedical progress in 5–10, conditional on regulatory and physical bottlenecks breaking. darioamodei.com/essay/machines-of-loving-grace . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-amodei-machines-of-loving-grace-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  206. Dave Carroll, “United Breaks Guitars,” YouTube, Jul 2009; Carroll, United Breaks Guitars: The Power of One Voice in the Age of Social Media (Hay House, 2012); Bloomberg market-cap analysis, Jul 2009. Cited in: Ch 19
  207. Dave Chaffey and Fiona Ellis-Chadwick, Digital Marketing , 8th ed. (Pearson 2022), Ch 9 attribution-maturity ladder; Smart Insights 2024-2026 RACE Growth System update; SOSTAC planning sequence and 5S/seven Cs frames. Synthesis in core-course/wiki/external/canonical/chaffey-digital-marketing-ch18.md . Cited in: Ch 18
  208. Davenport, “Competing on Analytics,” HBR , Jan 2006; Davenport and Harris, Competing on Analytics , HBS Press, 2007 (updated 2017); Davenport, Harris, Morison, Analytics at Work , HBR Press, 2010; IIA, “DELTA Plus Model and Five Stages of Analytics Maturity”; Stage 5 cases: Harrah’s, Capital One, Amazon, Marriott, Netflix, the Red Sox. Cited in: Ch 24
  209. David A. Aaker, Building Strong Brands (Free Press, 1996). Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/aaker-brand-identity-positioning-ch06.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  210. David Meerman Scott, The New Rules of Marketing and PR , 9th ed. (Wiley, 2024), and Newsjacking: How to Inject Your Ideas into a Breaking News Story and Generate Tons of Media Coverage (Wiley, 2011); the buy-beg-publish triad as the practitioner-tradition predecessor of the textbook PESO model, dating to the 2007 first edition; the ninth edition treats generative AI as the most significant development since 2007 and routes the publish-as-asset thesis into the AI-search era. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/meerman-scott-newsjacking-ch18.md . Cited in: Ch 18
  211. Demis Hassabis, “Accelerating Scientific Discovery with AI,” Nobel Lecture in Chemistry, Aula Magna, Stockholm University, December 8, 2024 ( nobelprize.org/prizes/chemistry/2024/hassabis/lecture ; lecture slides PDF at nobelprize.org/uploads/2024/12/hassabis-lecture.pdf ); Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 awarded jointly to Demis Hassabis and John Jumper (Google DeepMind) “for protein structure prediction” alongside David Baker for “computational protein design” ( nobelprize.org/prizes/chemistry/2024/hassabis/facts ); Isomorphic Labs writeup of the lecture ( isomorphiclabs.com/articles/nobel-prize-lecture ). David Silver and Richard Sutton, “Welcome to the Era of Experience,” April 26, 2025, 17-page essay excerpted from MIT Press Designing an Intelligence ( deepmind-media/Era-of-Experience ); Richard Sutton launch post ( x.com/RichardSSutton ); VentureBeat enterprise-prep framing ( venturebeat.com ); AlignmentForum technical commentary on grounded rewards ( alignmentforum.org ); Noumenal commentary on the grounded-reward content problem ( noumenal.ai/post/grounded-rewards ); The Decoder, January 2026, on Silver's departure from Google DeepMind and the founding of Ineffable Intelligence in November 2025 ( the-decoder.com ). The marketing-relevant excerpt that anchors the click-versus-trust illustration in this section: “a marketing campaign can look successful by clicks while damaging trust, a molecule can look promising in simulation and fail in a lab, a code agent can pass visible tests while introducing a security flaw”; the failure pattern across all three is short-horizon-reward optimization producing long-horizon cost. Reader synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/hassabis-nobel-deepmind-era-of-experience-ch27.md . Cited in: Ch 27
  212. Dietvorst, Simmons, and Massey, "Algorithm Aversion," JEP: General , 144(1), Feb 2015, 114-126. Cited in: Ch 4
  213. Disney-Reliance JioStar JV closing, Nov. 14, 2024 ($8.5B enterprise value, Reliance 63.16% / Disney 36.84%): JioStar press release; Deadline, Nov. 14, 2024. Disney+ Hotstar Q1 FY23 ~4M subscriber loss and Disney+'s 2.4M global net loss: Variety, Feb. 2023. Viacom18 IPL 2023-2027 digital-rights bid (rupees 23,758 crore): June 2022 BCCI auction. JioCinema IPL 2023 ad revenue (rupees 3,239 crore): India Startup News. JioHotstar Feb. 14, 2025 launch (300,000+ hours of content in 19 languages): Outlook Business. ARPU divergence (Disney+ global ~$4.40 vs Disney+ Hotstar India ~$0.59): Disney earnings releases. Synthesis at wiki/external/frontier/disney-jiostar-reliance-jv-2024-2026-ch15.md . Cited in: Ch 15
  214. Disneyland Paris corporate communications and Euro Disney SCA financial statements 1992-2026; Sebastien Brion, “Euro Disney's Cross-Cultural Stumble,” and business-press accounts of the 1992 launch and 1994 restructuring. Cited in: Ch 15
  215. Distilled-32B reasoning pricing, May 2026: Together.ai, Fireworks, DeepInfra, Replicate per-million rate cards on the DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B checkpoint, against OpenAI o1 list ($15 input / $60 output). Distilled R1 checkpoints (1.5B–70B) released MIT-licensed alongside the January 20, 2025 R1 paper. Capability-equivalent inference cost on reasoning tasks dropped roughly 50x in 16 months. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-the-reasoning-cost-shock-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  216. Dixon, M., & McKenna, T. (2022). The JOLT Effect: How High Performers Overcome Customer Indecision . Portfolio. Dataset: 2.5M+ coded B2B sales conversations. Anchors: 40-60% of B2B losses are no-decision; 56/44 split between indecision and status-quo preference; expectation-setting +155%, downside-minimization +109% (deployed on only 14% of calls), seller-recommendation +144% on the indecisive subsample. Cited in: Ch 23
  217. Dixon, M., Channer, R., Freeman, K., & McKenna, T. (2024). The Activator Advantage: What Today’s Rainmakers Do Differently . HBR Press. Rainmaker Genome Project: ~3,000 professional-services partners across 40+ firms in six sectors; five-profile taxonomy (Expert, Confidant, Debater, Realist, Activator) with Activator the only profile producing structural revenue growth; commit/connect/create triad; 15-20% natural-disposition share, trainable across the other four profiles. Cited in: Ch 23
  218. Dixon, Matthew, & Adamson, Brent. (2011). The Challenger Sale . Portfolio. Cited in: Ch 23
  219. Donald A. Norman, The Design of Everyday Things , Revised Edition (Basic Books, 2013), Chs 1–2: affordances, signifiers, constraints, mappings, feedback. Cited in: Ch 8
  220. Donald G. Reinertsen, The Principles of Product Development Flow (Celeritas, 2009): cost of delay, queues, batch size, WIP constraints, cadence/synchronization. Cited in: Ch 8
  221. Donald J. Bowersox, David J. Closs, M. Bixby Cooper, and John C. Bowersox, Supply Chain Logistics Management , 6th ed. (McGraw-Hill, 2024), ISBN 978-1-265-07260-5, canonical U.S. source for the integrated-logistics framework (customer accommodation, manufacturing support, procurement), the five logistics operational components (order management and customer service, inventory management, transportation, warehousing and materials handling, network design), the performance-cycle unit of logistics analysis (mean lead time, variance, cost), and the total-cost-analysis tradition (transportation, inventory carrying, warehousing, order-processing-and-information-systems, and lot-quantity costs minimized as a sum rather than line by line). The line originated as Donald J. Bowersox and David J. Closs, Logistical Management: The Integrated Supply Chain Process , 4th ed. (McGraw-Hill, 1996), ISBN 0-07-006883-6; the 1961 Bowersox-Smykay Physical Distribution Management (McGraw-Hill) is widely cited as the first firm-level treatment of physical distribution as a coherent discipline. Donald J. Bowersox (1932-2019), Eli Broad Distinguished University Professor at Michigan State, is widely referred to in the field as the “grandfather of supply chain.” Cited in: Ch 11
  222. Douglas M. Lambert, James R. Stock, and Lisa M. Ellram, Fundamentals of Logistics Management (Irwin/McGraw-Hill, 1998), ISBN 978-0-256-14117-7 (hardback) and 978-0-07-115752-0 (international student edition), xxiv + 611 pages. Canonical United States source for the marketing-perspective framing of logistics, the seven-element customer-service taxonomy organized into pre-transaction elements (written customer-service policy, accessibility, organizational structure, system flexibility, management services), transaction elements (stockout level, order information, order cycle, expediting, transshipment, system accuracy, order convenience), and post-transaction elements (installation, warranty, parts replacement, product tracing, customer complaints, claims, returns), the total-cost-analysis ladder (transportation, warehousing, order-processing and information, lot-quantity, inventory carrying cost), and the marketing-logistics interface. Lambert’s subsequent work through the Global Supply Chain Forum at Ohio State formalized the eight cross-functional supply-chain-management processes (customer relationship management, customer service management, demand management, order fulfillment, manufacturing flow management, supplier relationship management, product development and commercialization, returns management), restated in Douglas M. Lambert, ed., Supply Chain Management: Processes, Partnerships, Performance , 4th ed. (Supply Chain Management Institute, 2014). Predecessor volume: James R. Stock and Douglas M. Lambert, Strategic Logistics Management , 4th ed. (Irwin/McGraw-Hill, 2001), ISBN 978-0-256-13687-6. First European edition: Fundamentals of Logistics Management, First European Edition (McGraw-Hill, 2006). Cited in: Ch 11
  223. Douglas W. Hubbard, How to Measure Anything: Finding the Value of Intangibles in Business , 3rd ed. (Wiley, 2014), introduction to value-of-information reasoning underlying CLV decisions. Cited in: Ch 2
  224. Douglas W. Hubbard, How to Measure Anything: Finding the Value of Intangibles in Business , 3rd ed. (Wiley, 2014). Cited in: Ch 5
  225. Douglas W. Hubbard, How to Measure Anything: Finding the Value of Intangibles in Business , 3rd ed. (Wiley, 2014). The discipline of attaching every claim to a real source. Cited in: Ch 1
  226. Doyle Dane Bernbach, Think Small Volkswagen campaign 1959-1969; Frank Rowsome, Think Small: The Story of Those Volkswagen Ads (Stephen Greene Press, 1970); Karl Ludvigsen, Battle for the Beetle (Bentley Publishers, 2000), US sales figures across the 1960s. Cited in: Ch 16
  227. Doyle, Peter, Value-Based Marketing , op. cit., Chs. 3-5 on shareholder-value math, the discounted-cash-flow form of marketing decisions, and the ROI-vs-ROAS distinction. Cited in: Ch 25
  228. Doyle, Peter, Value-Based Marketing: Marketing Strategies for Corporate Growth and Shareholder Value , 2nd ed. (Wiley, 2008), Ch. 1 on the marketing-finance interface as the discipline’s strategic frontier. Cited in: Ch 25

  231. Drew Sanocki, The Triple Path to Profits , self-published 2018; Sanocki's operating frameworks documented across the Drew Sanocki Substack and the Empire Flippers podcast, 2018-2024. Cited in: Ch 13
  232. Duani, Barasch, Morwitz, “Demographic Pricing in the Digital Age,” JACR 9, 3 (2024): 257-268. Four experiments (n=276-469): algorithm-set discrimination rated more fair than human-set; serial mediation agency→intent→unfairness; boundaries: temporal-discrimination attenuation, prosocial-goal reversal. Synthesis at frontier/duani-barasch-morwitz-algorithmic-fairness-2024-ch10.md . Cited in: Ch 10
  233. dunnhumby Retailer Preference Index Q1 2025; Inside Trader Joe's podcast, “ICYMI: Loyalty Programs,” 2024. Cited in: Ch 6

Tappers tapped 25 melodies; predicted ~50% recognition; listeners identified 2.5% (3 of 120). Cited in: Ch 7

  245. Eli Schwartz, Product-Led SEO: The Why Behind Building Your Organic Growth Strategy (Houndstooth Press, 2021), ISBN 978-1-5445-1956-2; supply-side keyword strategy; Stripe / Airbnb / Notion / Zillow product-led cases. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/schwartz-product-led-seo-ch18.md . Cited in: Ch 18
  246. Ellis, S., & Brown, M. (2017). Hacking Growth: How Today’s Fastest-Growing Companies Drive Breakout Success . Currency. Origin of the “growth hacker” term (Ellis blog post, April 2010, Dropbox era); the North Star Metric framework with Airbnb “nights booked,” Spotify “time spent listening,” Slack “messages sent in active teams” as canonical instances; the Sean Ellis test for product-market fit (40 percent threshold on the “how disappointed would you be if you could no longer use this product” question); the vanity-metric definition convergent with Eric Ries (2011), The Lean Startup , Crown Business. Cited in: Ch 23
  247. eMarketer Dec 2025 (US TikTok Shop $15.82B; in-app conversion 5-8% vs 1-2%); EFulfillment 2025 (micro-creator engagement 30.1% under 50K). Synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/tiktok-shop-social-commerce-2025-ch19.md . Cited in: Ch 19
  248. eMarketer, “Retail Media Ad Spending Forecast Trends H2 2025”; Internet Retailing, “A Tale of Three Retail Media Behemoths,” 2025. Cited in: Ch 12

(O'Reilly, 2023); the relevance-authority-intent triangle, the four-stage keyword research workflow, and the first SEO bible to fold GEO into the core curriculum alongside E-E-A-T, Core Web Vitals, and the Helpful Content update. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/enge-spencer-stricchiola-art-of-seo-ch18.md . Cited in: Ch 18
  250. Eric Ries, The Lean Startup (Crown Business, 2011); Steve Blank, The Four Steps to the Epiphany (2005); Cooper and Sommer, “The Agile–Stage-Gate Hybrid Model,” JPIM 33:5 (2016). Cited in: Ch 8
  251. Erik Brynjolfsson. (2022, Spring). The Turing Trap: The Promise and Peril of Human-Like Artificial Intelligence. Daedalus , 151(2), 272–287. Substitution technologies replicate a human task and replace the worker (labor-cost line falls; productivity gain accrues to capital); augmentation technologies complement the worker (revenue line rises; gain available to labor as bargaining position). Procurement environment rewards substitution by default. Empirical anchor: roughly 9 dollars of intangible-capital investment (training, workflow redesign, organizational restructuring) required per dollar of machine-learning investment for the augmentation payoff to materialize, drawn from the IT-investment cycle of 1995–2010. Productivity-J-curve mechanism: complementary investment shows zero or negative measured returns for ~5 years before the gains arrive. direct.mit.edu/daed/article/151/2/272 . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-brynjolfsson-turing-trap-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  252. ESOMAR Online Sample Quality Guideline, September 2025; Cint Research Quality Center 2025 report; Dynata 2024 Data Quality Whitepaper; CloudResearch 2024-2025 panel benchmark. Cited in: Ch 5
  253. EU Digital Markets Act: Regulation (EU) 2022/1925 , enforceable March 6, 2024; EC IP/25/1085, April 23, 2025 (Apple €500M, Meta €200M); €2.95B Google ad fine, Sept. 2025. Synthesis in wiki/external/frontier/eu-dma-marketing-2024-2026-ch15.md . Cited in: Ch 15
  254. EU Digital Services Act: Regulation (EU) 2022/2065 , fully applicable February 17, 2024 (VLOPs designated August 2023). 25 designated VLOPs and VLOSEs. Maximum fine 6% of global annual revenue per breach. First non-compliance fine: €120M against X, December 2025 (deceptive blue-tick verification, inadequate ad repository, insufficient researcher data access); preliminary findings against TikTok (May 15, 2025, ad-repository transparency), Temu (Jul. 28, 2025, illegal-product risk), AliExpress binding commitments accepted (Jun. 18, 2025), TikTok and Meta (Oct. 24, 2025, researcher data access). TikTok ad-repository binding commitments accepted Dec. 5, 2025 with required 24-hour publication. Article 25 dark-pattern ban scope extended in the Digital Fairness Act draft expected Q3 2026. Synthesis in wiki/external/frontier/eu-dsa-marketing-2024-2026-ch15.md . Cited in: Ch 15

Article 101 ceiling: 3 percent worldwide turnover or €15M, effective August 2, 2026. Cited in: Ch 26

Google AI Mode 2024-2026: I/O 2025 launch (Pichai/Reid keynote); 75M DAU by Dec 2025; query fan-out across 8-12 sub-queries; 161% citation lift on main+fan-out wins (iPullRank); 93% zero-click on AI Mode vs 43% on AI Overviews (Semrush, Sept 2025); 134-167-word optimal extraction (AirOps, April 2026); schema nesting +40% after the March 2026 core update; Agarwal-Sen RCT (ISB/CMU, n=1,065, AEA registry, Jan-Feb 2026). Gourville, “Eager Sellers and Stony Buyers,” HBR 84, 6 (2006): 99-106 (9x effect: 3x seller overvaluation × 3x buyer loss aversion plus incumbent endowment; four-quadrant product-change × behavior-change typology with TiVo, Sonicare, original iPhone).   309. Grocery Dive, “Trader Joe’s store growth 2025” (2025); IBISWorld and Zippia, FY2024 revenue; Worldly Partners 2025; QuestionPro grocery NPS 2025; dunnhumby US Retailer Preference Index ; Trader Joe’s case bible at core-course/wiki/cases/trader-joes-case-bible.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  310. Heidary, Van der Rest, Custers, “Discrimination grounds and personalised pricing: Consumer perceptions of fairness, norm alignment, legality, and trust in markets,” Internet Policy Review 13, 4 (2024), DOI 10.14763/2024.4.1809. Dutch survey n=727, October 2024; twenty-five segmentation bases ranked on a seven-point fairness scale plus four parallel scales (personal-norm alignment, social-norm alignment, perceived legality, trust in digital markets). Equivalence-band finding: intelligence (M=2.52) and physical appearance (M=2.71) score within 0.3 points of legally prohibited grounds (religion M=2.27, nationality M=2.41, gender M=2.41, race M=2.44); no basis crosses the scale midpoint 4.0. Four pre-registered hypotheses confirmed at p<.001, including H4 immutability differential independent of legal status. Trust-erosion top three: political views M=5.35, nationality M=5.30, intelligence M=5.29. Companion supply-side study: Heidary, Custers, Pluut, Van der Rest, “A qualitative investigation of company perspectives on online price discrimination,” Computer Law and Security Review 47 (2022). Synthesis at frontier/heidary-vanderrest-custers-personalised-pricing-grounds-2024-ch10.md . Cited in: Ch 10
  311. Heinz It Has To Be Heinz , Rethink Toronto, 2024 Cannes Creative Effectiveness Grand Prix; DALL-E 2 prompts produced Heinz-shaped bottles without naming the brand. WARC Cannes 2024 Effectiveness. Mechanism: Rombach et al., "High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models," CVPR 2022; OpenAI 4o Image Generation, Mar 2025; Midjourney v7 --sref Apr 2025. Course-internal syntheses: frontier/ai-image-gen-creative-ch16.md ; frontier/ai-hartmann-superhuman-marketing-imagery-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16
  312. Helen Nissenbaum, "Privacy as Contextual Integrity," 79 Wash. L. Rev. 119 (2004); Privacy in Context (Stanford UP, 2009). Cited in: Ch 26
  313. Herb Sorensen, Inside the Mind of the Shopper: The Science of Retailing (Wharton School Press, 2009). Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/sorensen-inside-mind-shopper-ch12.md . Cited in: Ch 12
  314. Hermann Simon, Confessions of the Pricing Man (Springer, 2015), Chs 1-7 on the three pricing dimensions (level, structure, process) and the 11 percent profit-lift benchmark across roughly 10,000 Simon-Kucher engagements; Dolan and Simon, Power Pricing (Free Press, 1996); 2024 Simon-Kucher Global Pricing Study (~2,500 senior executives, 50 countries; intended-to-realized margin gap ~60 percent on average from sales-force discounting). Synthesis at canonical/simon-confessions-ch10.md . Cited in: Ch 10
  315. Hershey Company / Nestlé brand archives, Kit Kat US revenue arc 2007–2009; Berger and Schwartz, op. cit., on the cue-pairing mechanism. Cited in: Ch 19
  316. Hinterhuber, “Customer Value-Based Pricing Strategies: Why Companies Resist,” JBS 29, 4 (2008): 41-50 (five-barrier resistance paper, 486 citations); Liozu, Hinterhuber, Boland, Perelli, “Conceptualization of Value-Based Pricing in Industrial Firms,” JRPM 11, 1 (2012): 12-34; Liozu, Hinterhuber, “CEO Championing of Pricing, Pricing Capabilities and Firm Performance,” IMM 42, 4 (2013): 594-607 (n=748, 23 industries; 4-7 pp operating-margin gap top vs bottom CEO-championing quartile); Liozu, Hinterhuber, “Pricing Capabilities: Scale Design,” Management Decision 52, 1 (2014): 144-158; Hinterhuber, Liozu, Pricing Strategy Implementation (Routledge, 2019). Synthesis at canonical/hinterhuber-liozu-vbp-ch10.md . Cited in: Ch 10

  322. HubSpot, “Topic Clusters: The Next Evolution of SEO,” HubSpot Research, 2017; Backlinko (Brian Dean), “The Skyscraper Technique,” backlinko.com/skyscraper-technique ; Eli Schwartz, Product-Led SEO: The Why Behind Building Your Organic Growth Strategy (Houndstooth Press, 2021). Cited in: Ch 17
  323. IAB Incrementality Buyer’s Guide (Oct 2024); Microsoft Advertising incrementality (Jan 2026); Amazon AMC; Meta GeoLift; Google Trimmed Match; Brodersen CausalImpact; Haus; Measured; HelloFresh. Cited in: Ch 24
  324. Iacobucci, Dawn (Ed.). (2022). Marketing Strategy , 8th Edition. Cengage Learning. Cited in: Ch 23
  325. Iannarino, A. (2017). The Lost Art of Closing: Winning the Ten Commitments That Drive Sales . Portfolio. Ten-commitment ladder (time, explore, change, collaborate, build consensus, invest, review the solution, resolve concerns, decide, execute) as the operational definition of “qualified opportunity” and the spine that organizes Schiffman, Rackham, and Voss into stage-specific techniques. Cited in: Ch 23
  326. Iason Gabriel, "Artificial Intelligence, Values, and Alignment," Minds and Machines 30 (2020): 411-437. Cited in: Ch 26
  327. ICPEN/GPEN, Internet Sweep on Dark Patterns Affecting Subscription Services and Privacy , July 2024 (76% of 642 sites deployed at least one dark pattern, 27 jurisdictions). EU DSA Article 25 in force Feb 2024; FTC v. Amazon Prime $2.5B settlement Sept 2025. Cited in: Ch 4
  328. Inditex Annual Report 2024 and FY2024 / H1 2025 results presentations; Bloomberg , FT , WSJ , and Reuters coverage of Shein 2024-2025; Business of Fashion and Supply Chain Management Review 2024 benchmarks on the Zara cycle; HBS Working Knowledge on the Inditex test-and-respond model. Cited in: Ch 11
  329. Influencer Marketing Hub, Influencer Pricing Benchmarks 2026 , January 2026; Aspire, Brand-Creator Pricing Index , Q4 2025. Cited in: Ch 20
  330. InfoTrust, "Google's About-Face on Third-Party Cookies"; CDT, "Google's Privacy Sandbox Is Dead." infotrust.com ; cdt.org . Cited in: Ch 18

  332. Inside Trader Joe's podcast, “ICYMI: Loyalty Programs,” 2024; dunnhumby Retailer Preference Index, Q1 2025 grocery rankings. Cited in: Ch 5
  333. Iterable. 2025 Push Notification Benchmarks . Iterable, 2025. Opt-in rates, segment-level open rates, frequency benchmarks. Cited in: Ch 14
  334. Iterable. In-App Messaging Best Practices 2025 . Iterable, 2025. The three-form taxonomy: onboarding modals, contextual prompts, paywalls. Cited in: Ch 14
  335. ITSMA, Demandbase, & 6sense industry reports on account-based marketing, 2018-2025. Cited in: Ch 23
  336. J. Romaniuk, Better Brand Health: Measures and Metrics for a How Brands Grow World (Oxford University Press, 2023). The metric replacement stack and the operationalization of category-entry-point link strength as a brand-tracking instrument. Frontier reader: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/romaniuk-better-brand-health-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  337. J.-N. Kapferer, The New Strategic Brand Management , 5th ed. (Kogan Page, 2012). Brand Identity Prism: six-facet model (physique, personality, culture, relationship, reflection, self-image) along sender/recipient and externalized/internalized axes; originated 1986. The prism is most useful for luxury and identity-driven brands, Aaker for utility and B2B. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/kapferer-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  338. Jack Trout and Steve Rivkin, Differentiate or Die: Survival in Our Era of Killer Competition (Wiley, 2000). Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/trout-rivkin-differentiate-or-die-ch06.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  339. James E. Grunig and Todd Hunt, Managing Public Relations (Holt, Rinehart, and Winston, 1984), four-models framework; Public Relations Society of America (PRSA), “About Public Relations: Definition,” 2012, prsa.org/about/all-about-pr ; Robert L. Heath (ed.), Encyclopedia of Public Relations , 2nd ed. (SAGE, 2013). Cited in: Ch 22
  340. Jan Carlzon, Moments of Truth (Ballinger, 1987). Canonical moments-of-truth framing and front-line empowerment principle; see ref-20 for full SAS turnaround narrative. Cited in: Ch 9
  341. Jan Carlzon, Moments of Truth (Ballinger, 1987); English translation of Riv pyramiderna! (Bonniers, 1985). Source for the SAS turnaround (1981 CEO appointment, $17M annual loss to $54M first-year profit against a $2B industry-wide loss), the 50-million-moments calculation (10M customers x 5 employee contacts x 15 seconds), the EuroClass launch, Putting People First, and the inverted-pyramid frame. Cited in: Ch 9
  342. Jenni Romaniuk, Better Brand Health (Oxford, 2023), Chs. 2-3 (mental-availability metrics, panel workflow); Mark Ritson Marketing Week columns, January-March 2025, on the seven-asset distinctive-asset audit; Pollfish and Prolific 2024-2026 panel-pricing pages. Cited in: Ch 2
  343. Jim Meehan, The PDT Cocktail Book (Sterling Epicure, 2011); Please Don't Tell, New York City, opened 2007. Cited in: Ch 19
  344. Joe Pisani, “Amazon Says Its AI Shopping Assistant Rufus Is So Effective,” Fortune , November 2, 2025 (Rufus 250M users in 2025; MAU +140 percent YoY; interactions +210 percent YoY; engaged-shopper conversion +60 percent; $10B attributed incremental annualized sales on a seven-day downstream-impact window); Walmart Corporate, “The Future of Shopping Is Agentic. Meet Sparky,” June 6, 2025 (AOV +35 percent among engaged users; Wally merchant assistant March 2025; ChatGPT Instant Checkout October 2025); Target Corporate, “Target Launches New AI-Powered Features,” November 12, 2025 (Target Gift Finder, List Scanner, enhanced Store Mode; ~50 percent higher in-store basket size for app-active visitors); Walmart Corporate, “Walmart and Google Turn AI Discovery Into Effortless Shopping,” January 11, 2026 (Universal Commerce Protocol, Sam’s Club inventory, account-linked personalization); Retail Dive, “A Look at Retail’s Year of AI News,” 2025 (Adobe AI-driven retail traffic +758 percent YoY Nov 1-Dec 1 2025; Salesforce ~20 percent of global Cyber Week orders attributed to AI or agent influence). Frontier reader: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-walmart-sparky-rufus-target-2024-2026-ch12.md . Cited in: Ch 12
  345. Joe Pulizzi, Content Inc. , 2nd ed. (McGraw-Hill, 2021), and Epic Content Marketing , 2nd ed. (McGraw-Hill, 2023); the six-step audience-first model (Sweet Spot, Content Tilt, Base, Audience, Diversification, Monetization) plus the 4-1-1 social rule. CMI's official taxonomy now includes generative engine optimization as a recognized content category. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/pulizzi-content-inc-ch18.md . Cited in: Ch 18
  346. Joe Pulizzi, Content Inc.: How Entrepreneurs Use Content to Build Massive Audiences and Create Radically Successful Businesses , 2nd ed. (McGraw-Hill, 2021); UBM acquisition of Content Marketing Institute, June 2016, industry trade reporting. Cited in: Ch 17
  347. John A. Howard and Jagdish N. Sheth, The Theory of Buyer Behavior (John Wiley, 1969); Howard, Consumer Behavior in Marketing Strategy (Prentice Hall, 1989). Seminal integrative model: input streams, perceptual and learning constructs, outputs modulated by exogenous variables. Three-level decision typology (EPS, LPS, RRB) drawn from 19 longitudinal panel studies (1965-1968) across five packaged-goods categories. Cited in: Ch 4
  348. John A. Ledingham and Stephen D. Bruning, "Relationship Management in Public Relations: Dimensions of an Organization-Public Relationship," Public Relations Review , 24(1), 1998, 55-65; Ledingham and Bruning (eds.), Public Relations as Relationship Management: A Relational Approach to the Study and Practice of Public Relations (Lawrence Erlbaum, 2000). Linda Childers Hon and James E. Grunig, Guidelines for Measuring Relationships in Public Relations , Institute for Public Relations, November 1999, instituteforpr.org/measuring-relationships . The six relationship indicators: control mutuality, trust, satisfaction, commitment, communal relationships, exchange relationships. Wiki synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/ledingham-bruning-opr-ch22.md . Cited in: Ch 22
  349. Johnson & Johnson, 1982 annual report; Tylenol cyanide-poisoning case file. Recall scope (31M capsules), cost ($100M+), pre-crisis 37% share in Copulsky, "Brand Resilience," in Chernev & Kotler eds., Kellogg on Marketing , 3rd ed. (Wiley, 2023), Ch. 13. Recovery contrasted with Perrier and Gerber in Kotler, Keller, Chernev 16e, Ch. 10. Cited in: Ch 7
  350. Jonah Berger, Contagious: Why Things Catch On (Simon & Schuster, 2013). Cited in: Ch 19
  351. K. L. Keller, Strategic Brand Management , 5th ed. (Pearson, 2020); CBBE pyramid: salience, performance, imagery, judgments, feelings, resonance. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/keller-cbbe-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  352. Kahneman and Tversky, "Prospect Theory," Econometrica , 47(2), March 1979, 263-292. Cited in: Ch 4
  353. Kahneman, Thinking, Fast and Slow (FSG, 2011). System 1 / System 2 terminology from Stanovich and West, BBS , 23(5), 2000, 645-665. Cited in: Ch 4
  354. Karen Nelson-Field, The Attention Economy and How Media Works (Palgrave Macmillan, 2020); CARM project comparative attention studies, 2022-2024. Cited in: Ch 20
  355. Karen Nelson-Field, The Attention Economy and How Media Works (Springer, 2020); A Category Blueprint (Springer, 2024); VCCP Media × Nelson-Field, Hacking the Attention Economy (May 2025); IAB/MRC Attention Measurement Guidelines , Nov 2025. Amplified Intelligence aSeconds hierarchy and the $198B annual waste figure are drawn from Nelson-Field (2024) and WARC/ThinkTV 2024 attentive-reach analyses. Lumen Research's webcam-eye-tracking panel (37 countries, billions of impressions) supplies the 30%-of-viewable-ads-actually-viewed finding, the aCPM standard, and the 2.5-second memory-encoding floor; the IAB/MRC Nov 2025 framework assigns Lumen to digital display and OOH, Adelaide to CTV and audio, Amplified Intelligence to online video and broadcast. Course-internal syntheses: canonical/nelson-field-advertising-attention-ch16.md ; frontier/nelson-field-attention-economy-ch16.md ; frontier/attention-measurement-2025-vccp-adelaide-iab-ch16.md ; frontier/lumen-research-acpm-attention-2023-2025-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16
  356. Karen Nelson-Field, The Attention Economy: A Category Blueprint (Amplified, 2024); VCCP Media and Amplified, “Hacking the Attention Economy: The 1.5-Second Formula for Effective Digital Advertising,” May 2025; eye-tracking and biometric panel data. Synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/nelson-field-attention-economy-ch19.md . Cited in: Ch 19
  357. Karen Nelson-Field, “The CARM Project: Cross-Platform Attention Measurement,” University of South Australia, 2022-2024 publications; Amplified Intelligence, Attention Economy Annual Report 2024 . Cited in: Ch 20
  358. Karl Albrecht and Ron Zemke, Service America! (Dow Jones-Irwin, 1985). U.S. codification of the SAS turnaround that imported moments-of-truth into American services teaching two years before the English Carlzon, and source of the service triangle (customer at center; service strategy, systems, and people as corners) that became the dominant operating diagram of the field. Cited in: Ch 9
  359. Karl T. Ulrich and Steven D. Eppinger, Product Design and Development , 7e (McGraw-Hill, 2020). Stuart Pugh, Total Design (Addison-Wesley, 1990). Pugh selection (Ch 8), product architecture (Ch 9), prototyping (Ch 12), DFM (Ch 13), DCF (Ch 16). Cited in: Ch 8
  360. Karpathy, “Software Is Changing (Again),” YC AI Startup School, June 18, 2025. The utility-fabrication-operating-system analogy: a frontier model has the economics of a metered electricity utility, the capex profile of a semiconductor fab, and the orchestration role of a 1960s-era operating system simultaneously. Karpathy verbatim: “New LLM OS, fabbed by labs, and distributed like utilities (for now).” Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-karpathy-software-3-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  361. Kaushik, Web Analytics 2.0 , Wiley, 2010 (touchpoint-method limits); Kaushik, “See, Think, Do, Care,” Occam’s Razor , July 2013; MMM trade literature 2023-2026 on MMM as 2026 budget spine. Cited in: Ch 24
  362. Kevin Lane Keller, Strategic Brand Management , 5th ed. (Pearson, 2020), POP/POD architecture; Starbucks bull's-eye. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/keller-pop-pod-positioning-ch06.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  363. Khalsa, Mahan. (1999). Let’s Get Real or Let’s Not Play: The Demise of Dysfunctional Selling and the Advent of Helping Clients Succeed . FranklinCovey Sales Performance Group, amazon.com/Lets-Get-Real-Play-Dysfunctional . Khalsa, Mahan with Randy Illig. (2008, October 30). Let’s Get Real or Let’s Not Play: Transforming the Buyer/Seller Relationship , foreword by Stephen R. Covey. Portfolio (Penguin), penguinrandomhouse.com/books/303084 ; amazon.com/Lets-Get-Real-Not-Play . The book documents the ORDER discovery sequence (Opportunity, Resources, Decision-process, Exact-solution, Results) and the up-front contract framing for the start of every meeting, and is the canonical reference for the FranklinCovey Helping Clients Succeed training program. FranklinCovey course page at franklincovey.com/courses/helping-clients-succeed-strikingly-different-selling reports the program-data backdrop: roughly seventy percent of business-to-business sales meetings judged unsuccessful by the buyers in the room, seventy-eight percent of buyers seeing no or minimal difference between competing vendors, and an average global win rate of approximately seventeen percent for deals over one hundred thousand dollars. FranklinCovey press release at ir.franklincovey.com/news-releases covers the 2008 expansion release. Author biography at penguinrandomhouse.com/authors/239783/mahan-khalsa ; Trusted Advisor interview at trustedadvisor.com/trustmatters/trust-sales-and-getting-real . Secondary-source coverage of the ORDER mechanics: book summary at sellingsherpa.com/2021/01/18/lets-get-real-or-lets-not-play-book-summary ; book review at kimtasso.com/book-review-lets-get-real-or-lets-not-play ; chapter at oreilly.com/library/view/mastering-the-world/9780470617861/ch24.html . FranklinCovey South Asia curriculum outline PDF at franklincoveysouthasia.com/pdf/Curriculum/Helping Clients Succeed/Helping_Clients_Succeed_Course_Outline.pdf documents the one-day intensive and flipped-classroom delivery formats and the forty-plus countries the program has been delivered in. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/khalsa-illig-lets-get-real-ch23.md . Cited in: Ch 23
  364. Kim Scott, Radical Candor: Be a Kick-Ass Boss Without Losing Your Humanity (St. Martin's Press, 2017; revised second edition 2019). First Round Review, “Radical Candor: The Surprising Secret to Being a Good Boss,” 2015. radicalcandor.com. Scott career arc: Google operations leadership 2004–2009 (AdSense, YouTube, DoubleClick); Apple University faculty 2009–2014; Radical Candor LLC founder 2017. Cited in: Ch 8
  365. Klarna corporate communications, “Klarna AI Assistant Handles Two-Thirds of Customer Service Chats in Its First Month,” February 27, 2024; CMSWire and Big Technology, September 2025 (post-walkback hybrid: two-thirds AI chat coverage retained across 23 markets and 35+ languages, the four-rule production specification); Bloomberg, “Klarna Turns from AI to Real Person Customer Service After CEO Shift,” May 8, 2025; The Guardian , May 9, 2025. The canonical 2024-2026 reference case for agentic-AI customer-service deployments paired with the empowerment-and-recovery layer the failure period forced into the open. See ref-33 for the parallel Moffatt v. Air Canada tribunal record. Cited in: Ch 9
  366. Klarna investor and earnings disclosures, 2024-2025, on the Decagon-powered customer-service deployment handling roughly two-thirds of inbound chats at 700-FTE-equivalent throughput; HubSpot Spotlight Spring 2026 announcement, April 14, 2026, on outcome-based pricing for the Customer Agent ($0.50 per resolved conversation) and Prospecting Agent ($1 per qualified lead). Cited in: Ch 18
  367. Klarna Press, May 2024; Marketing Dive , FinTech Magazine , May 2024; ~$10M annual savings, 25 percent agency-spend cut, 6-week-to-7-day image cycle, 80 percent AI-drafted. Cited in: Ch 24
  368. Klarna walkback: Siemiatkowski Bloomberg interview, May 8-9, 2025; CNBC May 14, 2025; Klarna F-1, September 9, 2025 (38 percent headcount reduction attributed to AI); toolkit synthesis at frontier/klarna-walkback-operational-data-ch27.md . Cited in: Ch 27

  400. Kwakyi, G. Incipia ASO Framework . Incipia, 2023-2025. The canonical practitioner manual on App Store Optimization, with three-axis keyword optimization across traffic, difficulty, and relevance. Cited in: Ch 14
  401. L. Binet and P. Field, The Long and the Short of It (IPA, 2013); 996 IPA Effectiveness Awards campaigns over 30 years. Penetration-led campaigns ~3x as effective as loyalty-led at producing measurable business effects. Cited in: Ch 7
  402. LangChain Inc. Series B (October 20, 2025, $125M at $1.25B; 90M monthly SDK downloads; 35 percent of Fortune 500); LangChain 1.0 / LangGraph 1.0 GA October 22, 2025 (durable execution, typed state graphs); langchain-mcp-adapters bridge ( langchain-mcp-adapters ); State of Agent Engineering 2026 (1,340 respondents, 57.3 percent in production); toolkit synthesis at frontier/ai-langchain-langgraph-langsmith-2024-2026-ch27.md . Cited in: Ch 27
  403. Larry Light and Joan Kiddon, Six Rules for Brand Revitalization (Pearson / FT Press, 2009; second edition 2017 as Six Rules of Brand Revitalization ); New Brand Leadership: Managing at the Intersection of Globalization, Localization and Personalization (Pearson, 2016); The Paradox Planet: Creating Brand Experiences for The Age of I (Archway, 2017); Larry Light, The Fourth Wave: Brand Loyalty Marketing (Coalition for Brand Equity / 4A's, 1994); Joan Kiddon and Larry Light, “The Resurgence of Brand Loyalty,” Branding Strategy Insider; Larry Light, “There Is More to Brand Loyalty Than Behavior” and “How Brands Can Address Multiple Customer Segments,” Branding Strategy Insider; Larry Light, “Aim for Brand Loyalty: Brand Loyal Customers Are More Price Tolerant,” LinkedIn Pulse. The corpus develops the two-dimensional (behavioral + attitudinal) loyalty thesis, the differentiated-loyalty position (segment-specific loyalty engineering under one brand promise), and the McDonald's 2002-2006 Plan to Win turnaround as the empirical anchor (Q4 2002 $343.8M loss to 32 consecutive months of positive comps and a stock move from ~$12 to ~$45 by 2006). Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/light-resurrection-of-brand-loyalty-ch12.md . Cited in: Ch 12
  404. Lehmann, Winer, Product Management , 4e (McGraw-Hill, 2004), Ch 10 Pricing Decisions (four-procedure reservation-price taxonomy: direct asking, Van Westendorp PSM, conjoint, scanner-data revealed-preference; end-benefit pricing; price as information signal); Lehmann, Winer, Analysis for Marketing Planning , 7e (McGraw-Hill, 2008), pricing-and-product chapter (analytical companion). Synthesis at canonical/lehmann-winer-product-management-ch10.md . Cited in: Ch 10

  438. Manolo Iachizzi (Griffith University), “Brewing crises: Bud Light’s stakeholders’ loyalty and crisis communication fizzle,” Media International Australia (2025, online; DOI 10.1177/1329878X251337819). The first peer-reviewed retrospective applying Coombs’ Situational Crisis Communication Theory to the 2023 Bud Light boycott via Instagram outrage content analysis (52.63 percent trust as dominant outrage factor, 44.74 percent stakeholder control, 70 percent humor-and-sarcasm in share-prone reactions) and Anheuser-Busch InBev’s executed Bolstering response posture. Sales and market-share figures cross-confirmed by CNN, CBS News, Newsweek, Brauwelt, and Accio coverage 2024-2025: ~40 percent sustained sales decline through February 2025, loss of the twenty-two-year U.S. top-selling-beer position to Modelo Especial in May 2023, more than $1 billion in U.S. revenue loss in the first nine months. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/iachizzi-bud-light-2024-2026-ch02.md . Cited in: Ch 2

(Oxford University Press, 2017), Ch 5 ("The Marketing Environment") on the Kodak digital-photography misread (line 6611) as the canonical missed-shift companion to Blockbuster (line 6617); Steven J.   463. Moffatt v. Air Canada , 2024 BCCRT 149 (BC Civ. Resol. Trib., February 14, 2024), CanLII 2024bccrt149 , holding the airline liable in negligent misrepresentation for chatbot-returned bereavement-fare information, awarding $650.88 in damages plus $36.14 pre-judgment interest and $125 in tribunal fees, and rejecting the “separate legal entity” defense; Joshua Krook in AI & Society (2024). Cited in: Ch 9
  464. N. Ind, Living the Brand , 3rd ed. (Kogan Page, 2007); N. Ind ed., Branding Inside Out (Kogan Page, 2017). The four-stage internal-branding cycle (Discover, Define, Develop, Deliver) operationalizes the Schultz/Hatch corporate-branding diagnostic at the employee-rendering layer. Empirical replication: Punjaisri and Wilson, JBM (2007, 2009, 2011); King and Grace, JBM (2010): employee brand-citizenship behavior mediates internal-branding activity to customer-perceived brand strength. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/ind-living-the-brand-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  465. N. Klein, No Logo: Taking Aim at the Brand Bullies (Knopf Canada, 1999; Picador, 2000). Four pressure points organize the critique: No Space (commercial saturation of public and quasi-public space), No Choice (consolidation of media and retail behind a small number of holding firms), No Jobs (labor outsourcing through asset-light contractor chains), and No Logo (the resistance movement). 1988 inflection: Phil Knight's framing of Nike as a marketing firm whose product is its most important marketing tool, treated as the moment brand became the firm's primary asset and the factory became a contractor relationship the firm could deny. 2024-2026 update record: 2013 Rana Plaza building collapse (1,134 garment-worker deaths, Bangladesh); EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (Directive (EU) 2024/1760, in force July 2024, phased application 2027-2029); California SB-261 climate-related financial-risk disclosure and SB-253 climate corporate-data accountability act (signed October 2023, effective 2026); UK Modern Slavery Act 2015 §54 and Australia Modern Slavery Act 2018 reporting requirements; Shein and Temu as the speculative end-state of the 1999 model now operating at scale. Co-opted-resistance prediction confirmed across Absolut label-less campaigns, Starbucks Reserve outlets stripped of the corporate logo, and the post-2020 indie-sublabel pattern run by major holding companies; the 2026 form extends to AI-generated faux-handcraft packaging visuals and AI-generated faux-personal social voice. AI-era extension: data-labor outsourcing, dataset-rights opacity, and content-creator attribution stripped at the model layer follow the same contractual-distance pattern the 1999 critique documented for physical goods. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/klein-no-logo-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  466. Nagle and Müller, The Strategy and Tactics of Pricing , 6e (Routledge, 2018). Cited in: Ch 10

Peppers and Rogers, The One to One Future (Doubleday/Currency, 1993), the share-of-customer pivot; Enterprise One to One (Doubleday, 1997), introducing the IDIC methodology; Managing Customer Relationships (Wiley, 2004; 3rd ed. 2016). Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/peppers-rogers-one-to-one-ch02.md . Cited in: Ch 2
  519. Peter F. Drucker, The Practice of Management (Harper, 1954), Ch. 5; the “purpose of a business is to create a customer” formulation. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/levitt-drucker-ch02.md and the 2026 update at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/drucker-create-a-customer-2026-update-ch02.md . Cited in: Ch 2
  520. Peter Field, The Crisis in Creative Effectiveness (IPA, June 2019), 24-year analysis of ~600 IPA Effectiveness Awards case studies (1996-2018); Field and Binet, Selling Creativity Short (IPA, 2016) on the same dataset's earlier window. Headline findings: creative-multiplier collapse from ~12x (period ending 2008) to ~4x (2018) to short-horizon parity (2024 IPA Databank refresh); evaluation-horizon shift from 10% of campaigns at six months or less in 2002 to 25% in 2018; FMCG optimum brand share of 76% against industry under-allocation of at least 14 points. The empirical foundation for ref-33, ref-38, and ref-39. Course-internal synthesis: frontier/field-crisis-creative-effectiveness-2019-2024-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16
  521. Peter S. Fader, Bruce G. S. Hardie, and Michael Ross, The Customer-Base Audit (Wharton School Press, 2022), Ch 2 “The Data Cube and the Five Lenses” for Lens 3 cohort decomposition (descriptive retention by tenure / spend percentile before predictive models); Fader, Customer Centricity , 2nd ed. (Wharton Digital Press, 2020); see core-course/wiki/concepts/customer-base-audit-five-lenses.md for the Lens 3 retention-curve diagnostic this RFM tier-mix simulator operationalizes. Cited in: Ch 12
  522. Pettibone and Wedell, "Examining Models of Nondominated Decoy Effects," OBHDP , 81(2), March 2000, 300-328; "Testing Alternative Explanations of Phantom Decoy Effects," JBDM , 20(3), July 2007, 323-341. Cited in: Ch 4
  523. Phil Barden, Decoded: The Science Behind Why We Buy (Wiley, 2013), ISBN 978-1-118-34560-2; second edition 2020 with foreword by Rory Sutherland of Ogilvy UK, ISBN 978-1-119-67308-8 (oreilly.com/library/view/decoded-2nd-edition/9781119673088/; decoded-book.com/). The translation of the Kahneman dual-process tradition into brand-briefing vocabulary: autopilot (System 1) and pilot (System 2); explicit and implicit value as the two axes of perceived value (perceived value = perceived reward minus perceived pain, with reward and pain each split across explicit and implicit axes); the decision interface as the gating layer at the moment of choice; the four operational levers of signaling, anchoring, framing, and peripheral cues. Connection to the modern attention literature: Karen Nelson-Field's Amplified Intelligence panel research, including the 2024-2025 VCCP Media collaboration revising the 2.5-second memory-encoding threshold downward to 1.5 seconds when distinctive assets are present (vccp.com/uk/news/2025/may/hacking-the-attention-economy-vccp-media-and-dr-karen-nelson-field-reveal-1-5-second-formula-for-effective-digital-advertising; uk.themedialeader.com/just-1-5-seconds-is-enough-for-ad-recall-new-attention-study-reveals/). Practitioner summaries used in the synthesis: Alex Murrell, "Phil Barden, Decoded" (alexmurrell.co.uk/summaries/phil-barden-decoded); Tapestry Works, "Decoding Consumer Behaviour" (tapestry.works/2020/04/11/decoding-consumer-behaviour-review-and-summary-of-decoded-by-phil-barden/); Marketing-Psycho, "Marketing Decoded" (marketing-psycho.com/marketing-decoded/); NMSBA, "Decoded: The Science Behind Why We Buy" (nmsba.com/neuromarketing/books/decoded-the-science-behind-why-we-buy). Course-internal synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/barden-decoded-neuromarketing-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16
