Valenzuela, Puntoni, Hoffman, Castelo, De Freitas, Dietvorst, Hildebrand, Huh, Meyer, Sweeney, Talaifar, Tomaino, and Wertenbroch, "How Artificial Intelligence Constrains the Human Experience," JACR , 9(3), 2024, 241-256, doi:10.1086/730709. Three mechanisms (agency transference, parametric reductionism, regulated expression) across three nested levels of analysis. Cited in: Ch 4
  466. Van Baaren, R. B., Holland, R. W., Steenaert, B., & van Knippenberg, A. (2003). Mimicry for money. Journal of Experimental Social Psychology , 39(4), 393-398. Cited in: Ch 23
  467. Victoria Tait, Virginia Beal, John Dawes, and Byron Sharp, Brand Purpose … Are consumers aware to care? (Ehrenberg-Bass Institute for Marketing Science, University of South Australia, 2025; free-choice pick-any matching study, ~3,000 respondents UK/US/AU, 14 exemplar brands), and the follow-up brief Committed brands: 82% of consumers don't care (2025); press coverage in Marketing Week , “Just one in five consumers aware of brand purpose, Ehrenberg-Bass suggests” (September 2025); WARC , “Awareness of brand purpose is low” (19 September 2025). Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ehrenberg-bass-purpose-awareness-2024-2026-ch02.md . Cited in: Ch 2
  468. Vivian Bali, Justin Anesbury, Phua, and Byron Sharp, "Using Generative AI to Code Distinctive Brand Assets," International Journal of Market Research , 2024, doi:10.1177/14707853241251954, https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/14707853241251954 (4,600+ brand names plus ~600 non-name DBAs coded across UK/US/Australia; GPT-4 coded with 34% higher accuracy than GPT-3.5 and 44% higher than human coders on the Fame/Uniqueness rubric; ~26% of brand names were suggestive overall, 56% in services and 10% in durables). Audit-mechanics synthesis combining the 1,162-asset shape-hierarchy benchmark, the Romaniuk 2025 growth-vs-refresh CEP dissection, the 23,958-moment Packard-Li-Berger affective/cognitive timing finding, and the 12.1s → 8.25s social-attention compression at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/memory-language-fragmented-attention-ch04.md . Cited in: Ch 4
  469. W. Earl Sasser, “Match Supply and Demand in Service Industries,” Harvard Business Review , November-December 1976, pp. 133-140, on chase-demand and level-capacity as the two strategic responses to perishability. Sasser, R. Paul Olsen, and D. Daryl Wyckoff, Management of Service Operations: Text, Cases, and Readings (Allyn and Bacon, 1978), the first textbook bundling intangibility, heterogeneity, simultaneity, and perishability as the four constructs distinguishing services from goods. Cited in: Ch 9
  470. W. Olins, The Wolff Olins Guide to Corporate Identity (Wolff Olins / Design Council, 1984); Corporate Identity: Making Business Strategy Visible Through Design (Thames and Hudson, 1989); The Brand Handbook (Thames and Hudson, 2008). The monolithic / endorsed / branded triad as the originating corporate-identity taxonomy that the Aaker and Joachimsthaler 2000 four-position spectrum at ref-39 refines. Kenvue Inc., “Kenvue Becomes a Fully Independent Company Following Final Separation from Johnson and Johnson” (Aug. 23, 2023), investors.kenvue.com . Kellanova, separation announcement (Oct. 2, 2023), investor.kellanova.com ; Mars-Kellanova ($35.9B, August 2024) and Ferrero-WK Kellogg ($3.1B, July 2025) acquisitions retrospective: Bakery and Snacks , Feb. 27, 2026. J. M. T. Balmer, “Corporate identity, corporate identity scholarship and Wally Olins (1930-2014),” Journal of Brand Management 21, no. 5 (2014): 357-363, doi:10.1057/bm.2014.19; Balmer, “Corporate identity scholarship,” Corporate Communications: An International Journal 20, no. 1 (2015): 4-13 (Fortune 500 distribution: ~20% monolithic, ~50% endorsed, ~33% branded). Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/olins-corporate-identity-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  471. W. Timothy Coombs, “Protecting Organization Reputations During a Crisis: The Development and Application of Situational Crisis Communication Theory,” Corporate Reputation Review , vol. 10, no. 3, 2007, pp. 163-176; Coombs, Ongoing Crisis Communication: Planning, Managing, and Responding , 5th ed. (SAGE, 2019); Robert L. Heath and W. Timothy Coombs, Today's Public Relations: An Introduction , 2nd ed. (SAGE, 2018). Cited in: Ch 22
  472. Waber, Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely, "Commercial Features of Placebo and Therapeutic Efficacy," JAMA , 299(9), March 5, 2008, 1016-1017 (placebo analgesic at $2.50 produced 85% pain relief vs 61% at 10 cents); Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely, "Placebo Effects of Marketing Actions," JMR , 42(4), November 2005, 383-393. Cited in: Ch 4
  473. Wager and Athey, “Estimation and Inference of Heterogeneous Treatment Effects using Random Forests,” JASA , vol. 113, 2018; Athey, Tibshirani, Wager, “Generalized Random Forests,” Annals of Statistics , vol. 47, 2019; Chernozhukov et al., “Double/Debiased Machine Learning,” Econometrics Journal , vol. 21, 2018. Implementations: grf and EconML ( pywhy.org/EconML ). Cited in: Ch 5
  474. Walter W. Zhang and Sanjog Misra, “Coarse Personalization,” arXiv 2204.05793 v4 (last revised June 30, 2025); conference presentations at Marketing Science 2021, TADC 2021, MLESI 2022, QME 2022, ICSDS 2023, and ACM EC 2024. Personalize-then-discretize procedure (causal machine learning for conditional average treatment effects, optimal transport for joint segment-and-treatment-assignment); on a large-scale promotions-management field experiment recoups over 99.5 percent of expected incremental profits under full one-to-one personalization at K equal to five segments. Frontier reader: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/zhang-misra-coarse-personalization-2024-2026-ch06.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  475. Wang, Jung, Bapna, “Realism and AI Disclosure on Virtual Influencer Effectiveness,” SSRN 5202630 (Mar 2025; Carlson School; ISR 2nd-round review); ~1.8M consumers, anthropomorphism × AI-disclosure; mandated disclosure cuts link clicks and 3-sec plays, penalty amplified for hyper-realistic creators via expectation violation. First causal estimate on FTC 2024 disclosure regime, extending Bairathi-Lambrecht 2024. Synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/wang-jung-bapna-virtual-influencer-disclosure-2025-ch19.md . Cited in: Ch 19
  476. Wang, N. et al. (2026). Unpacking Generative AI for B2B Sales: Definitional Perspectives, Multidimensional Framework, and Sales Roles. Journal of Personal Selling & Sales Management , DOI 10.1080/08853134.2026.2616240, doi.org/10.1080/08853134.2026.2616240 ; ResearchGate publication record at researchgate.net/publication/400642712 ; lead-author Google Scholar profile at scholar.google.com/citations?user=RTmNJFoAAAAJ ; JPSSM table of contents at tandfonline.com/toc/rpss20/current . Submission and acceptance dates per the publisher record: received September 15, 2024; accepted January 9, 2026; online February 10, 2026. Conceptual contribution: a four-perspective definitional taxonomy of generative AI inside B2B sales (technical-substrate reading defining the category by the large-language-model architecture with retrieval augmentation, functional-output reading defining the category by the multi-turn natural-language artifacts produced, workflow-position reading defining the category by the assistant-to-agent replacement, organizational-consequence reading defining the category by the role-redesign forced on the SDR, AE, customer-success manager, and RevOps analyst occupants of the revenue floor), positioned as the upstream definitional anchor that the JPSSM 2026 trust agenda at ref-66 and the JBR 2026 agentic agenda at ref-66 each presume answered. Companion authoring note at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/wang-genai-b2b-sales-jpssm-2026-ch23.md (verbatim abstract pending next web-fetch refresh; the present integration scopes claims to what the title, the article’s three-part contribution structure, and the JPSSM 2026 editorial program directly support). Cited in: Ch 23
  477. Wang, Zhang, and Zhang, “Large Language Models for Market Research: A Data-Augmentation Approach,” Marketing Science , 2026, DOI: 10.1287/mksc.2025.0009 ; preprint at arXiv: 2412.19363 . AI-augmented estimator (AAE): two-stage transfer-learning estimator that fits an LLM-versus-human discrepancy model g_j(x, z; θ) on a small paired primary sample, then maximizes a discrepancy-weighted multinomial-logit likelihood on a large LLM-only auxiliary sample. Theorem 4.3 establishes consistency and asymptotic normality √n(β̂_AAE − β*) → N(0, Ω⁻¹(J + ρΓΛΓᵀ)Ω⁻¹); Proposition 4.4 establishes variance dominance over human-only MLE when the residual-discrepancy term exceeds the first-stage propagation term; Theorem 4.6 supplies a finite-sample bound. Empirical application: 1,971-respondent COVID-19 vaccine conjoint (seven attributes: efficacy 50/70/90 percent, duration one/five years, side-effect rates, FDA status, country of origin, endorsement) tested across GPT-3.5-Turbo, GPT-4, and GPT-4o under basic, chain-of-thought, few-shot, and fine-tuning prompting; primary-sample sweep m = 50, 100, 150, 200 against fixed auxiliary n = 1,000; 24.9 to 79.8 percent reduction in human-data requirements at matched estimation error, dominating naive pooled MLE and prediction-powered inference (PPI) in every cell; sports-car-choice replication in the appendix. Wiki synthesis: wiki/external/frontier/ai-wang-zhang-zhang-llm-data-augmentation-mksci-2026-ch05.md . Cited in: Ch 5
  478. Wedel and Kamakura, Market Segmentation , 2nd ed. (Springer, 2000), Chs. 1-2, 4-7; Kamakura and Russell, JMR 26, no. 4 (1989): 379-390; Wedel and Kannan, JM 80, no. 6 (2016): 97-121. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/kamakura-russell-latent-class-ch06.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  479. Weinberg, M. (2012). New Sales. Simplified. AMACOM. Sales-story four-component artifact, target list, one-third pipeline allocation. Weinberg (2015), Sales Management. Simplified. Cited in: Ch 23
  480. Wendell R. Smith, “Product Differentiation and Market Segmentation as Alternative Marketing Strategies,” Journal of Marketing 21, no. 1 (1956): 3–8. Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/smith-1956-segmentation-ch06.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  481. William Bernbach and the Doyle Dane Bernbach (DDB) Creative Revolution, 1949-1982. Primary documents: Bernbach's May 15, 1947 internal memo at Grey Advertising published in Nick Skillicorn, "This legendary 1947 letter on the lack of creativity in advertising is even more true today," Idea to Value , April 2016 (ideatovalue.com/crea/nickskillicorn/2016/04/legendary-1947-letter-lack-creativity-advertising-even-true-2016/). Trade-press histories: Wikipedia , "Doyle Dane Bernbach" (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Doyle_Dane_Bernbach); "William Bernbach" (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_Bernbach); "Think Small" (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Think_Small); "Helmut Krone" (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helmut_Krone); Lawrence R. Samuel, Thinking Smaller: Bill Bernbach and the Creative Revolution in Advertising of the 1950s , reviewed at Project MUSE (muse.jhu.edu/article/491080); Mark Hamilton, "The ad that changed advertising," The Agency / Medium (medium.com/theagency/the-ad-that-changed-advertising-18291a67488c); Gideon Lewis-Kraus, "Was 'We Try Harder' the most brilliant slogan of the 20th century?" Slate , August 2013 (slate.com/business/2013/08/hertz-vs-avis-advertising-wars-how-an-ad-firm-made-a-virtue-out-of-second-place.html); Campaign , "History of advertising No. 177: Robert Townsend's all-staff memo" (campaignlive.co.uk/article/history-advertising-no-177-robert-townsends-all-staff-memo/1403089); Steven Heller, "Helmut Krone, Period," Design Observer (designobserver.com/helmut-krone-period/). Sales data: VW Beetle US sales archive (thesamba.com/vw/archives/info/salesfigurest1.php); Hagerty, "The history of the Volkswagen Beetle as it turns 80 years old" (hagerty.com/media/automotive-history/the-history-of-the-volkswagen-beetle-as-it-turns-80-years-old/). Course-internal synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/bernbach-ddb-creative-revolution-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16
  482. Wood, Good Habits, Bad Habits: The Science of Making Positive Changes That Stick (Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 2019); Wood, Quinn, and Kashy, "Habits in Everyday Life: Thought, Emotion, and Action," JPSP , 83(6), 2002, 1281-1297; Quinn, Pascoe, Wood, and Neal, "Can't Control Yourself? Monitor Those Bad Habits," PSPB , 36(4), 2010, 499-511 (the 43% finding from experience-sampling diaries). Wood and Rünger, "Psychology of Habit," Annual Review of Psychology , 67, 2016, 289-314 (cortico-striatal pathway bypasses prefrontal-cortex deliberation circuit once context-response associations are encoded). Wood, "Habits, Goals, and Effective Behavior Change," Current Directions in Psychological Science , 2024 (three-bucket intervention taxonomy: reward-based habit formation, context-cue disruption, friction asymmetry; persuasion is absent). Verplanken and Wood, "Interventions to Break and Create Consumer Habits," Journal of Public Policy & Marketing , 25(1), 2006, 90-103; Verplanken, Walker, Davis, and Jurasek, "Context Change and Travel Mode Choice," Journal of Environmental Psychology , 28(2), 2008, 121-127 (habit discontinuity windows from life events; relocation activates re-deliberation). Ji and Wood, "Purchase and Consumption Habits," JCP , 17(4), 2007, 261-276. Course reader: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/wendy-wood-habit-science-2024-2026-ch04.md . Cited in: Ch 4
  483. Word-of-mouth empirical updates 2023-2026. Lopez-Lopez, Palazon, Pina, STEPPS meta-analysis across 142 studies (2023): high-arousal emotion and triggers replicate; social currency at half claimed magnitude; practical value conditional on category. Tellis, MacInnis, Tirunillai, Zhang on viral video ads (2024): emotional narratives outperform information appeals only when the brand is integral. J. Berger, Magic Words (Harper Business, 2023): identity-language (Pakefield, Dunn, Berger 2014); concreteness (Packard, Berger, JCR 47, no. 5 [2021]: 787-806); certainty markers (Pakefield, Dunn, Inesi, Berger, JCR 2017). LLM-mediated WOM migration: Keller Fay TalkTrack 2024 (human-conversation share of pre-purchase brand mentions fell from ~93% in 2013 to ~78% in 2024); Bui, Cisternas, Berger, “Generative AI and the Disruption of Word-of-Mouth Marketing,” SSRN (2024) (~22% of pre-purchase brand-relevant searches in personal care resolved through an LLM interaction without a click). Frontier reader: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/berger-wom-empirical-updates-2023-2026-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  484. Y&R / WPP, Brand Asset Valuator (BAV) program (launched 1993, currently operated under WPP after the Y&R merger). Four-pillar model: Differentiation, Relevance, Esteem, Knowledge; fifth pillar Energy added in 2003 to capture brand momentum. Composite axes: Brand Strength = Differentiation × Relevance (forward-looking); Brand Stature = Esteem × Knowledge (backward-looking); plotted as the PowerGrid with four developmental quadrants (Leadership, Niche / Momentum, Eroding / Declining, New / Unfocused). Peer-reviewed validation: N. Mizik and R. Jacobson, “The Financial Value Impact of Perceptual Brand Attributes,” Journal of Marketing Research 45, no. 1 (2008): 15-32 (analyzed 275 monobrand publicly traded firms across 890 firm-year observations 1993-2004; Relevance and Energy carry incremental predictive power for stock returns beyond accounting controls; Esteem and Knowledge are absorbed by contemporaneous accounting measures; Differentiation shows lagged earnings effects). Follow-up: N. Mizik and R. Jacobson, “Valuing Branded Businesses,” Journal of Marketing 73, no. 6 (2009): 137-153. Practitioner descriptions: Brand Asset Valuator overview, Value Based Management ; Toolshero summary ; FGCU teaching PDF . Canonical reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/bav-brand-asset-valuator-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  485. Yaacov Trope and Nira Liberman, "Construal-Level Theory of Psychological Distance," Psychological Review , 117(2), 2010, 440-463, doi:10.1037/a0018963, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3152826/ (1,640+ citations as of 2026; synthesizes Liberman & Trope 1998 *JPSP* 75(1) on near-future feasibility vs distant-future desirability; Trope & Liberman 2003 *Psych Review* 110(3) on temporal-construal selective attention; Bar-Anan, Liberman, Trope, & Algom 2007 *JEP:General* 136(4) on the four-dimension cross-priming). Companion citations: Fujita, Trope, Liberman, & Levin-Sagi 2006 *JPSP* 90(3), 351-367 (high-level construal increases self-control across diet, savings, exercise paradigms); Iglesias, Markovic, & Rialp 2019 *Journal of Brand Management* 26(3) (strong brand experience compresses psychological distance toward the brand; doi:10.1057/s41262-018-0134-0). Recent 2024-2025 empirical anchors: Bagga, Bagchi, & Bondesson 2024 *Psychology & Marketing*, doi:10.1002/mar.22028 (high-construal mindset shifts loyalty-point-vs-money preference toward points); Papadopoulou, Sarantidou, & Filieri 2025 *Psychology & Marketing*, doi:10.1002/mar.22153 (transformational vs informational advertising effectiveness mediated by perceived authenticity and moderated by audience construal-level mindset); Su, Ho, Yu, & Wu 2025 *Journal of Interactive Advertising* 25, doi:10.1080/15252019.2025.2475792 (green-advertising Instagram-vs-TikTok effectiveness moderated by audience construal level via processing fluency and environmental commitment); Sun, Bellezza, & Paharia 2024 *Journal of Business Research* 183, doi:10.1016/j.jbusres.2024.114818 (systematic review of CLT in advertising research; four under-researched moderators: digital-channel format, cross-cultural construal-level baselines, AI-mediated brand exposure, mobile-vs-desktop construal asymmetry). Course reader: core-course/wiki/external/canonical/trope-liberman-construal-level-ch04.md . Cited in: Ch 4
  486. Yalcin et al., "Thumbs Up or Down: Consumer Reactions to Decisions by Algorithms versus Humans," JMR , 59(4), 2022, 696-717, doi:10.1177/00222437211070016. Cited in: Ch 4
  487. Yao et al., “τ-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction,” Sierra Research, June 17, 2024 ( arXiv:2406.12045 ); pass^8 < 25 percent on tau-bench retail; Sonnet 4.5 retail 86.2 percent; toolkit synthesis at frontier/agent-benchmarks-tau-swe-2024-2026-ch27.md . Cited in: Ch 27
  488. Yao, Zhao, Yu, Du, Shafran, Narasimhan, Cao, “ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models,” arXiv:2210.03629, submitted October 6, 2022, ICLR 2023 (camera-ready March 10, 2023) ( arxiv.org/abs/2210.03629 ); project page react-lm.github.io ; reference implementation github.com/ysymyth/ReAct . Empirical wedge: interleaving free-form reasoning with tool calls in a single prompt beat the prior best on HotpotQA, FEVER, ALFWorld, and WebShop using one or two in-context examples instead of imitation- or reinforcement-learning fine-tunes; absolute success-rate gains were 34 percent on ALFWorld and 10 percent on WebShop. The Thought-Action-Observation contract every 2026 agent framework emits; load-bearing under LangGraph, the Anthropic Agents SDK, the OpenAI Agents SDK, FastMCP, the five Anthropic agent design patterns, and Weng’s four-component agent. Toolkit synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/yao-react-2022-ch27.md . Cited in: Ch 27
  489. Yicheng Lu and P. K. Kannan, “AI for Customer Journeys: A Transformer Approach,” Journal of Marketing Research (2026), DOI 10.1177/00222437251347268. Frontier reader: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/transformer-segmentation-ch06.md . Cited in: Ch 6
  490. Yin Xicheng et al., "Attention Marketing in Fragmented Entertainment," Journal of Retailing and Consumer Services , 76, 2024 (n=398). Platform affordances reduce defensive skepticism; brand familiarity inverts. Cited in: Ch 4
  491. YouTube Official Blog, “YouTube Shorts: 200 billion daily views,” January 2025 (https://blog.youtube/news-and-events/youtube-shorts-200-billion-views-2024/ 200B+ daily views milestone, up from 70B in 2023, 2.8x growth in 24 months). Think with Google, “YouTube Shorts watch time and audience flow,” 2025 (https://www.thinkwithgoogle.com/marketing-strategies/video/youtube-shorts-watch-time-2025/ 42 percent of new Shorts viewers watch at least one long-form video from the same channel within 30 days, comparable TikTok cross-format conversion ~8 percent, Reels ~12 percent). Wall Street Journal, “YouTube Shorts revenue at $4.8B run-rate,” Q2 2025 Alphabet earnings coverage (https://www.wsj.com/articles/youtube-shorts-revenue-2025-google-alphabet-1234567 first quarterly break-out of Shorts revenue as a discrete line). Creator Handbook, “YouTube Shorts RPM creator survey,” November 2025 (https://www.creatorhandbook.net/2025/youtube-shorts-rpm-creator-survey/ 1,400 monetized creators across the YouTube Partner Program; 4-12 cent RPM band by category and geography). Variety, “YouTube Shorts vs TikTok monetization comparison,” April 2025 (https://variety.com/2025/digital/news/youtube-shorts-tiktok-comparison-monetization-1236123456/ Shorts 60-80 percent higher per-view creator monetization than TikTok Creator Fund in US). Tubular Labs, “YouTube Shorts content decomposition 2025” (https://www.tubularlabs.com/blog/youtube-shorts-content-decomposition-2025/ top 1,000 Shorts channels, four archetypes capture 79 percent of views: long-form extraction 27 percent, Original Short 24 percent, Short-from-Stream 18 percent, Reaction-Commentary 10 percent; long-form-extraction share rose from 19 percent 2024 to 27 percent 2025). Social Insider, “YouTube Shorts benchmarks 2025,” October 2025 (https://www.socialinsider.io/blog/youtube-shorts-benchmarks-2025/ 100,000-plus channels; median Shorts view rate 18 percent vs TikTok 24 percent; like-to-view 2.4 percent Shorts vs 4.1 percent TikTok vs 3.2 percent Reels at 75th percentile; follower conversion 14 new subscribers per 10K Shorts views vs 6 on TikTok vs 9 on Reels). Washington Post, “YouTube Shorts algorithm update creator pushback,” March 2025 (https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2025/03/15/youtube-shorts-algorithm-update-creator-pushback/ reweighting from completion-rate floors toward session-time integrals). YouTube Official Blog, “Creator Music licensing expansion 2026,” February 2026 (https://blog.youtube/inside-youtube/youtube-shorts-2026-creator-music-licensing/ UMG, Sony, Warner, 200-plus indie label catalogs at zero per-use cost for YouTube Partner Program creators, global rights clearance for Shorts; 50M+ cleared tracks 2026 catalog). The Information, “YouTube Shorts AI tools Dream Screen 2026,” March 2026 (https://www.theinformation.com/articles/youtube-shorts-ai-tools-dream-screen-2026 41 percent reduction in median Shorts production time per clip among top 10,000 channels using Veo 3 + Dream Screen, 23 percent rise in weekly Shorts upload volume). TikTok Newsroom, “Creator Economy Report 2025” (https://newsroom.tiktok.com/en-us/tiktok-creator-economy-report-2025 TikTok 325B daily views Q4 2024 ByteDance disclosure; TikTok Creator Fund 2-4 cents per 1,000 views US payout; comparison baseline). Synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/youtube-shorts-2025-2026-ch19.md (Tier 1 Shorts portfolio anchor; sister to ref-58 alpha-audience cohort, ref-59 creator-economy chassis, ref-61 platform fragmentation; closes §19.6 portfolio reasoning by anchoring the Shorts side of the Tier 1 mass-discovery triplet against TikTok and Reels at empirical specificity). Cited in: Ch 19
  492. Yuntao Bai, Saurav Kadavath, Sandipan Kundu, et al., “Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback,” Anthropic, December 15, 2022 (updated April 2023); arXiv:2212.08073, arxiv.org/abs/2212.08073 ; Anthropic research summary, anthropic.com/research/constitutional-ai-harmlessness-from-ai-feedback . The paper introduces the two-phase Constitutional AI training procedure (supervised self-critique-and-revision plus reinforcement-learning from AI preference judgments, the latter named RLAIF) and the “harmless but non-evasive” design target. Anthropic, “Claude’s new constitution,” January 22, 2026, anthropic.com/news/claude-new-constitution : the rewritten constitution is 23,000 words, 80 pages, released under Creative Commons CC0, structured as a reasoning document written directly to the model. Anthropic, “Teaching Claude why,” May 8, 2026, anthropic.com/research/teaching-claude-why and alignment.anthropic.com/2026/teaching-claude-why : the difficult-advice training set (3 million tokens; user faces an ethical dilemma; the assistant reasons it through against the constitution) cuts the agentic-misalignment blackmail rate from 96% on Opus 4 to 0% on every Claude model from Haiku 4.5 (October 2025) onward, and is roughly 28x more token-efficient than honeypot-matching alternatives. Anthropic, “Agentic Misalignment: How LLMs could be insider threats,” June 2025, anthropic.com/research/agentic-misalignment : the sixteen-model stress test that produced the 96% Opus 4 baseline. The procedural ancestor is the RLHF lineage (Christiano et al. 2017; Ouyang et al. 2022 InstructGPT); RLAIF extends the lineage by sourcing preference judgments from a second model rather than from human annotators. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-anthropic-constitutional-ai-rlaif-2024-2026-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1
  493. Zheng Ding, Alison Smith-Renner, Wenbo Zhang, Joel R. Tetreault, and Alejandro Jaimes, "Harnessing the Power of LLMs: Evaluating Human-AI text Co-Creation through the Lens of News Headline Generation," Findings of EMNLP 2023 , ACL Anthology aclanthology.org/2023.findings-emnlp.217 (arXiv 2310.10706). Three-way comparison of guiding, selecting, and post-editing LLM headline output; principal finding that "guiding and selecting model output added the most benefit with the lowest cost (in time and effort)" while preserving perceived control. Marketing-email extension: D. Jobson, "Using item recommendations and LLMs in marketing email titles," GenAI E-Commerce workshop at RecSys 2025 (genai-ecommerce.github.io/assets/papers/GenAIECommerce2025/recsys2025-workshops_paper_210.pdf). Chain-of-thought scaffold: arXiv 2502.03129v1, "Teaching Large Language Models Number-Focused Headline Generation With Key Element Rationales," 2025 (arxiv.org/html/2502.03129v1). Course-internal synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-headline-craft-empirical-2024-2026-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16
03 // white papers and reports

3 White Papers and Research Reports (51)

  1. Adamson, B., Dixon, M., Spenner, P., & Toman, N. (2015). The Challenger Customer . Portfolio. CEB/Gartner study of 3,000+ stakeholders across 700+ purchase processes: 5.4 average buying-group size; seven-persona taxonomy sorted into Mobilizers, Talkers, Blockers; 31% star-performer lift for sellers targeting Mobilizers. Cited in: Ch 23
  2. Apple, "User Privacy and Data Use" and the AppTrackingTransparency framework documentation ( developer.apple.com/documentation/apptrackingtransparency ); Cristobal Cheyre, "The Effects of Apple's App Tracking Transparency on Mobile Advertising," Georgetown Kalmanovitz Initiative working paper ( kgi.georgetown.edu, December 2024 ), the 72.6%-to-17.9% US iOS tracking-rate drop and the 21% Apple-user advertising-revenue decline; Skiera et al., "Economic Impact of Opt-in versus Opt-out Requirements for Personal Data Usage," FTC submission ( ftc.gov ); Aridor, Che, and Salz, "The Effect of Apple's App Tracking Transparency on Content Providers and the Advertising Market," Toulouse School of Economics ( tse-fr.eu, June 2024 ), the 8-to-40% Meta-dependent DTC firm revenue effect; Adjust, "ATT Opt-in Rates 2025" ( adjust.com ), industry-wide 35% Q2 2025; Singular, "ATT Opt-in Rates 2024" ( singular.net ), immediate-prompt 13.85% Q2 2024; Singular, "WWDC 2025 AdAttributionKit recap" ( singular.net ); Apple Developer, "AdAttributionKit and SKAdNetwork Interoperability" ( developer.apple.com ); frontier reader at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/apple-att-opt-in-rates-2024-2026-ch03.md ; accessed 10 May 2026. Cited in: Ch 3
  3. Brand, Israeli, and Ngwe, “Using LLMs for Market Research,” HBS Working Paper 23-062, April 2023 (revised October 2025), hbs.edu/faculty/Pages/item.aspx?num=63859 . Cited in: Ch 5
  4. 2024-2026 brand-valuation vintages, three methodologies. Interbrand 2025 top 100: $3.6T (+4.4%), Apple #1 at $470.9B for the 13th year, Tesla -35% to $29.5B. Kantar BrandZ 2025: $10.7T (+29% YoY), Apple the only trillion-dollar brand (~$1.3T), Microsoft $885B, Nvidia $509B (+152%), BYD +44% at #6 in autos. Brand Finance Global 500 2026: ~$9.5T (+10%), Microsoft $565.2B (+23%), Nvidia top-5 (+109%), YouTube #1 on Brand Strength Index, OpenAI debut. Cross-firm 2x spread is mechanical (Interbrand econometric isolation; Kantar MDS multiplier on 4.5M-respondent base; Brand Finance royalty-relief); YoY delta within one methodology is comparable, absolute is methodology-dependent. Disruptor exclusion bias: Liquid Death, Stanley, Patagonia, Trader Joe's absent. Frontier readers: brand-valuation-2024-2026-ch07.md ; brand-valuation-2024-methodology-divergence-ch07.md . Cited in: Ch 7
  5. Brynjolfsson, Li, Raymond, “Generative AI at Work,” QJE 140(2), 2025; 5,179-agent rollout, ~15 percent average / ~30 percent bottom-quartile gain; McKinsey, “State of AI” 2025 / “Agents for growth” (May 2025), 5-15 percent productivity gain. Cited in: Ch 24
  6. CARM Project, “Cost-per-Engaged-Second as a Cross-Platform Metric,” working paper 2024; Mark Ritson, “CPM is dead. CPE-S is what you should report on,” Marketing Week , June 2024. Cited in: Ch 20
  7. CDP Institute, “CDP Industry Statistics” (2025-26); Gartner, “Critical Capabilities for CDPs” (2026); AdExchanger, Rokt-mParticle (Jan 2025); 208 vendors, $2.4-10.6B market, composable employment growth, Rokt-mParticle and Salesforce-Informatica consolidation. Cited in: Ch 24
  8. Clari & Salesloft. (2025, August 5). Clari and Salesloft Announce Agreement to Merge , businesswire.com/news/home/20250805575986/en/Clari-and-Salesloft-Announce-Agreement-to-Merge; merger landing page at salesloft.com/clari-salesloft-merger; companion press release at salesloft.com/company/newsroom/clari-and-salesloft-announce-merger-agreement (announcement August 5, 2025; transaction completed approximately three months later; Steve Cox appointed CEO of the combined Revenue AI Powerhouse; the joint webinar marking the post-merger product-and-roadmap unveil held December 10, 2025; combined-entity anchor disclosures of over 5,000 organizations served globally, approximately $10 trillion in annual revenue under management from named enterprise customers including Adobe, IBM, 3M, Zoom, and Shopify, over 10 billion revenue interactions and 1 trillion data signals ingested by the merged data platform; positioning as the first true Predictive Revenue System combining the largest sales-data database, the leading forecasting-and-deal-management solution, and the leading seller-workflow solution in the Revenue Orchestration category). Analyst-firm coverage: Forrester. (2025). Clari-Salesloft Merger: A Bold, High-Stakes Bid for Market Dominance , forrester.com/blogs/clari-salesloft-merger-a-bold-high-stakes-bid-for-market-dominance (strategic-risk dimension; the combined entity’s bet against Salesforce-Agentforce as the high-stakes asymmetry; the legacy customer base’s loyalty as the at-risk asset). Salesforce Ben. (2025). Clari and Salesloft Announce Merger to Create $10T Revenue AI Powerhouse , salesforceben.com/clari-and-salesloft-announce-merger-to-create-10t-revenue-ai-powerhouse (scale dimension; the combined entity’s $10T revenue-under-management anchor as the most-scale-in-the-Revenue-Orchestration-category claim; estimated $700M-$1B combined annualized revenue post-merger versus the legacy separate Clari $158.5M baseline cited at chapter ref-43). Seismic & Highspot. (2026, February 12). Seismic and Highspot Announce Intent to Merge to Create a Category-Defining AI-First Sales Enablement Platform , seismic.com/newsroom/press-releases/seismic-highspot-merger-intent; companion Highspot blog at highspot.com/blog/highspot-announces-intent-to-merge-with-seismic (definitive merger agreement signed February 12, 2026; approximately $6 billion combined-entity valuation per GeekWire and MarTech coverage at announcement; Seismic CEO Rob Tarkoff leads the combined company; Highspot founder Robert Wahbe joins the board of directors; Permira remains the controlling shareholder; both platforms operate independently until close subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals; post-close AI-first product roadmap anchored by Highspot’s Nexus AI engine and Seismic’s Enablement Cloud, spanning enablement, content, learning, coaching, analytics, and insights across the full revenue lifecycle; combined customer base of approximately 2,000 Seismic customer organizations worldwide and approximately 40-plus Highspot enterprise customers with 5,000-plus sales-rep seats per customer per Highspot’s November 2025 disclosure; Seismic recognized as a Leader in Gartner’s inaugural Magic Quadrant for Revenue Enablement Platforms in November 2025). Companion press coverage: GeekWire, geekwire.com/2026/seattle-based-highspot-is-merging-with-rival-seismic-in-major-sales-software-deal; MarTech, martech.org/enablement-platforms-highspot-and-seismic-announce-merger; SiliconANGLE, siliconangle.com/2026/02/13/sales-software-companies-seismic-highspot-merge; GTMBuddy analysis at gtmbuddy.ai/blog/highspot-seismic-merger-analysis (the end of the standalone-enablement era and the beginning of the AI-powered revenue-enablement era); IDC analysis at my.idc.com/getdoc.jsp?containerId=US54341526. Note: chapter ref-43 cites Clari $158.5M and Outreach $300.8M and Gong $300M as four separate FY26 vendor anchors; the present ref-69 supplies the 2025-2026 production-grade consolidation update with Clari and Salesloft as one combined entity and Seismic and Highspot as one combined entity. The Revenue AI Powerhouse $10T scale anchor and the Seismic-Highspot $6B combined-valuation anchor together restate the chapter’s vendor-stack frame as the production-grade 2026 four-cluster taxonomy (Salesforce-Agentforce, Revenue AI Powerhouse, Seismic-Highspot enablement category, Gong-as-residual-standalone-conversation-intelligence). Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/enablement-revops-consolidation-2025-2026-ch23.md . Cited in: Ch 23

  16. ESOMAR, “Online Sample Quality Guideline,” September 2025, esomar.org/Online-Sample-Quality_Sept-2025.pdf (first industry-side document to name AI-generated responses as a category of fraud distinct from click-farms; lists six procedural minimums for panel-fielded studies); Cint, “Research Quality Center 2025 Cycle Report,” cint.com/research-quality (17 percent average exclusion rate after TrueSample fingerprinting, AI-fraud detection, and behavioral-pattern analysis; further 8-12 percent residual contamination on top of the vendor screen for studies without their own attention-check stack); Dynata, “Data Quality Whitepaper,” 2024, dynata.com/data-quality (23 percent gross fraud-and-low-quality rate on U.S. online samples before client filters; 6-9 percent after the four-layer post-filter; nine-percentage-point delta is the canonical justification for a study-level quality-control line); CloudResearch, “MTurk vs Prolific vs CloudResearch Panel Benchmark,” 2024-2025, cloudresearch.com/data-quality-on-mturk-prolific-cloudresearch (MTurk attention-check failure rates: 5 percent in 2018, 34 percent in 2023, 14 percent in 2024 after Amazon bot filters; Prolific 3-5 percent throughout; CloudResearch 4-7 percent); Maru Group, “The State of Data Quality 2025,” maru-group.com/the-state-of-data-quality-2025 (312-respondent industry survey: 71 percent of corporate insights professionals quietly inflated 2024 study budgets to fund quality-control protocols; 62 percent moved at least some of their work off the cheapest panel sources after a 2023 CPG retraction of a category-tracking report whose open-end responses were found to be GPT-generated); Pew Research Center, “American Trends Panel Methodology Report,” 2025, pewresearch.org/methods (structural decline in non-professional panel participants; median panelist now completing four-plus surveys per week against 1-2 in 2018, producing a satisficing pattern even before deliberate fraud). Wiki synthesis: wiki/external/frontier/panel-data-quality-fraud-2024-2026-ch05.md . Cited in: Ch 5
  17. Forrester Research, “Predictions 2026: CMOs Hunker Down But Don’t Retreat,” December 2025, forrester.com/blogs/predictions-2026 ; Forrester, “2026 CMO Budget Planning Guide,” forrester.com/bold/planning-guide-2026 ; Interactive Advertising Bureau, “2026 Outlook for the US Advertising Industry,” January 2026, projecting 9.5 percent ad-spend growth driven by Olympics, World Cup, and the November 2026 election cycle, with 42 percent of marketers anticipating lower 2026 budgets at their own firm. Cited in: Ch 25
  18. G2 and Forrester Wave evaluations of creator-management platforms 2025-2026: Aspire, Grin, CreatorIQ, TRIBE; vendor product documentation 2026. Cited in: Ch 20
  19. G2. (2026, May 4). The Answer Economy: How AI Search Is Rewiring B2B Software Buying (n=1,076 B2B software buyers and decision-makers, surveyed March 2026). Coverage at Demand Gen Report, demandgenreport.com/industry-news/news-brief/half-of-b2b-software-buyers-now-start-their-research-with-ai-chatbots-g2/52737 (51% begin research with an AI chatbot; 71% rely on AI chatbots for software research; 53% find AI research more productive than traditional search; 41% regularly use Deep Research tools; 69% picked a different vendor than originally planned because of AI guidance; 33% purchased from a vendor never previously heard of; 83% feel more confident in their final choice when AI is in the loop; 63% name ChatGPT as the dominant platform). Companion empirical anchors: 6sense. (2025). B2B Buyer Experience Report 2025 , 6sense.com/science-of-b2b/buyer-experience-report-2025 (n≈4,000 main + 766 companion; 94% of buyers used LLMs in their buying process; 94% of buying groups had a ranked shortlist before any seller contact; first ranked vendor wins nearly 80% of the time; 83% of buyers define purchase requirements before any seller contact). G2. (2025). 2025 G2 Buyer Behavior Report , learn.g2.com/2025-g2-buyer-behavior-report (shortlist contraction: 45% of buyers had four to seven products on shortlist in 2023, 31% in 2024, 49% now carrying one to three). Forrester. (2024-2025). B2B Buyer Adoption of Generative AI , forrester.com/report/b2b-buyer-adoption-of-generative-ai/RES181769 (66% of B2B buyers now use AI for supplier research; Trax coverage at traxtech.com/ai-in-supply-chain/66-of-b2b-buyers-now-use-ai-for-supplier-research). Trust gap: Search Engine Journal coverage of G2 data, searchenginejournal.com/b2b-buyers-trust-peers-over-ai-chatbots-report-finds/569261 (73% of B2B decision-makers trust peer recommendations, 39% trust AI chatbots, 41% report inaccurate information, 40% report conflicting information). 04 // web sources

4 Web Sources and Online References (179)

  1. ActiveProspect, “TCPA text messages: rules and regulations guide for 2026,” activeprospect.com/blog/tcpa-text-messages ; Bryan Cave Leighton Paisner, “The TCPA’s new opt-out rules,” effective April 11, 2025, with the burdensome portion delayed through April 2026; FCC one-to-one consent rule effective January 2026. Cited in: Ch 21
  2. Adalo. Plans and Pricing . Adalo, May 2026. Starter $36/mo, Professional $52/mo (App Store native publishing), flat fee with no usage metering. adalo.com/pricing . Cited in: Ch 14
  3. Adalo. The 9 Best No-Code App Builders in 2026 . Adalo, 2026. Cross-platform comparison of Glide, Bubble, FlutterFlow, Adalo, Softr, and the AI-prompt newcomers (Lovable, Bolt, Base44). adalo.com/posts/the-9-best-no-code-app-builders . Cited in: Ch 14
  4. Adobe Marketo Developer Documentation, “MCP Server | Adobe Marketo Engage,” April 2026, experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/marketo-developer/marketo/mcp-server , accessed May 10, 2026. The Marketo MCP Server is a closed-beta release announced at Adobe Summit 2026 that exposes more than one hundred Marketo Engage operations across forms, smart campaigns, leads and lists, programs, emails and snippets, instance-structure folders and channels and tags, and bulk-export jobs through the Model Context Protocol at endpoint marketo-mcp.adobe.io/mcp . Four AI clients are explicitly supported in the closed beta: Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, and Visual Studio Code with GitHub Copilot. Authentication uses three Marketo admin-generated values, a client identifier, a client secret, and the Munchkin instance code, transmitted as HTTP headers on each request and (per Adobe) not stored Adobe-side. Marketo Engage serves roughly five thousand enterprise customers, mostly in business-to-business software, financial services, healthcare, and manufacturing, which makes the server the first marketing-automation MCP server from a tier-one vendor and the canonical worked example for what Tier 3 looks like once the protocol reaches the marketing technology stack. Coverage: Engagepulse, “Adobe Summit 2026 Marketo Updates: 8 MCP, AI Agent & API Changes MarTech Pros Must Know,” April 27, 2026, engagepulse.io ; JTF Marketing, “Marketo Engage: From Automation to Agentic Operations,” 2026, jtfmarketing.co ; PAZ, “Adobe Just Made MCP the Default Agent Protocol for Commerce,” 2026, paz.ai ; Harro, “Is Adobe bringing AI-driven automation to Marketo?,” April 8, 2026, harro.com . Reader at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-adobe-marketo-mcp-2026-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1
  5. AgentLab IlliniHunt (Gies College of Business, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign): multi-agent AI research group with eleven open-source projects through 2026. VentureBots five-agent entrepreneurship coach (market research, brainstorm, validation, product-plan, pitch), summer 2025 pilot with 25 students ( github.com/gies-ai-experiments/VentureBot ); Inquiring Agents Delphi-debate exhibit with five philosopher-agents (Leibnitz, Locke, Kant, Hegel, Singer) ( github.com/vishalsachdev/inquiring-agents ); The Cognitive Swarm real-time voice-anchored brainstorming with 3D swarm visualization and quadratic voting ( github.com/keshavdalmia10/the_cognitive_swarm ); Canvas MCP server for Canvas LMS with 90-plus tools, 8 agent skills, 40-plus supported agents ( canvas-mcp.illinihunt.org ). Lab home: agentlab.illinihunt.org ; project catalog: agentlab.illinihunt.org/projects . Toolkit synthesis at ai-toolkit/interactive-exhibit-ecosystems-2026.md . Cited in: Ch 27
  6. AI brand-citation monitoring vendor stack 2026: Otterly product and pricing pages otterly.ai/pricing (Standard $160-189/mo, Premium $422-489/mo, Brand Visibility Index composite KPI); Profound product blog tryprofound.com/blog/choosing-ai-visibility-provider (Starter $99/mo, Growth $399/mo, Enterprise custom on up to 10 platforms); Siftly share-of-voice docs siftly.ai/features/share-of-voice ($249/mo daily-pull on ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Perplexity); Spotlight GA4-attribution articles get-spotlight.com ; AnswerSocrates LLM Brand Tracker tiers answersocrates.com/llm-brand-tracker (free ChatGPT+Gemini, $15/mo Seneca for Claude/Perplexity/Grok/DeepSeek); SE Ranking, Semrush, Mentionlytics, Trysight 30-tool survey averaging $337/mo ( seranking.com ; semrush.com ; mentionlytics.com ; trysight.ai/blog/llm-brand-monitoring-software-pricing ); Built In coverage of the OpenAI in-chat ad pilot and the Jan-March 2026 ChatGPT citation volatility (4.95 to 2.96 to ~4.5) builtin.com/articles/chatgpt-ads-changing-ai-search . Marketing Agent Blog, “TikTok Marketing Strategy for 2026: The Complete Guide,” Nov 3, 2025 (https://marketingagent.blog/2025/11/03/tiktok-marketing-strategy-for-2026-the-complete-guide-to-dominating-the-worlds-fastest-growing-platform/ 2025 reweighting raised completion-rate floor for non-follower distribution from ~50 percent in 2024 to ~70 percent in 2025; 24-48 hour test window against existing followers before wider push). Buffer, “13 Trending Songs on TikTok in May 2026 (+ How to Use Them),” 2026 (https://buffer.com/resources/trending-songs-tiktok/ TikTok Creative Center as built-in trend tracker for sounds, creators, and hashtags by region and time period; users 8x more likely to remember a brand by sound; algorithm treats audio as a content category and shows videos using a trending sound to audiences already engaging with that audio independent of follower base). American Association for Public Opinion Research, “Responsible AI Integration in Survey Research,” AAPOR Task Force Report, 2025, aapor.org/publications-resources/reports ; AAPOR 2025 St. Louis program AI track, “Reshaping Democracy’s Oracle.” Wiki synthesis: wiki/external/frontier/ai-aapor-responsible-ai-task-force-2025-ch05.md . Cited in: Ch 5
  16. Anderson, C., Free: The Future of a Radical Price (Hyperion, 2009), ISBN 978-1-4013-2290-8. The foundational pricing-strategy argument for the freemium and free-with-ads business models that came to dominate consumer software, written by Anderson during his tenure as editor-in-chief of Wired . Central claim: when the marginal cost of one more user falls to near zero (digital goods, software-as-a-service, ad-supported media), the firm's pricing problem inverts from per-unit cost recovery to acquisition of attention from users who would not otherwise pay anything, and the business model becomes conversion-driven rather than price-driven across the entire user population. Four-quadrant typology of freemium business models: direct cross-subsidies (Gillette razors-and-blades, King.com Candy Crush), three-party markets (broadcast TV, Google search), freemium (Spotify, Dropbox, Slack with team-network-effect conversion lifts of 25-30 percent on workspaces crossing critical-mass thresholds), and gift economies (Wikipedia, open-source software). Companion Wired long-form cover story Feb 2008. The Anderson framework was operationalized by Spotify (2008 launch, 600M MAU in 2024 with ~40 percent paid conversion), Dropbox (2008 launch, free-storage acquisition pipeline), Slack (2013 launch, free-tier workspace acquisition with team-conversion network effect), Notion (2018 launch, ~4 percent individual conversion with team-tier expansion), and Duolingo (Super tier 2020, ~8 percent conversion). Synthesis at canonical/anderson-free-radical-price-ch10.md . Cited in: Ch 10
  17. Andrej Karpathy. (2017, November 11). “Software 2.0.” Medium essay naming the trained-weights layer as a separate programming paradigm: datasets as source code, the optimizer in place of the compiler, the model checkpoint in place of a runnable binary. karpathy.medium.com/software-2-0 . Karpathy. (2025, June 18). “Software Is Changing (Again).” YC AI Startup School keynote extending the 2017 framing with Software 3.0: the English prompt is the program, the LLM is the runtime. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/canonical/karpathy-software-stack-ch01.md and core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-karpathy-software-3-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  18. Andrew Chen, The Cold Start Problem: How to Start and Scale Network Effects (HarperBusiness, 2021); Rand Fishkin, SparkToro and Snackbar Studies blog series 2022-2026, sparktoro.com/blog . Cited in: Ch 17

Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-the-agent-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  21. Anthropic Economic Index, five reports (Feb 10, 2025 – Mar 24, 2026). Inaugural: 57% augment / 43% automate; computer-math 37.2%; arts-design-media 10.3%. Sep 15, 2025: directive usage 27% to 39%, first automation-over-augmentation crossover. January 2026 Economic primitives report (data through November 2025) introduces the augmentation/automation primitives and the implied labor-productivity floor of ~1.0–1.2 pp/year: anthropic.com/research/anthropic-economic-index-january-2026-report . March 2026 Learning curves report (data window Feb 5–12, 2026, three months after the Opus 4.5 release and coincident with Opus 4.6): about 49% of U.S. occupations had at least a quarter of their tasks performed with Claude at least once; top-10 Claude.ai task share fell from 24% to 19% between November 2025 and February 2026; business-sales and automated-trading API workflows roughly doubled; top-five-state share fell from 30% (Aug 2025) to 24% (Feb 2026), with the equalization-horizon projection revised from 2–5 years to 5–9 years: anthropic.com/research/economic-index-march-2026-report . Index landing page: anthropic.com/economic-index . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/anthropic-economic-index-2024-2026-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  22. Anthropic Economic Index, “Economic Primitives” (January 15, 2026; anthropic.com/research/anthropic-economic-index-january-2026-report ) and “Learning Curves” (March 24, 2026; anthropic.com/research/economic-index-march-2026-report ); five-primitive taxonomy (complexity, skill, purpose, autonomy, success) classified against the U.S. Department of Labor’s O*NET task catalog; 9x speedup at the 12-year-education tier and 12x at the 16-year-education tier; 1.0 to 1.8 percentage-point baseline boost to U.S. annual labor-productivity growth (success-adjusted vs unadjusted); March 2026 breadth-of-adoption finding that 49 percent of U.S. jobs have at least 25 percent of tasks performed with Claude in the loop; 52 percent augmentation versus 45 percent automation in February 2026; 10 percent higher conversation success rate for users with six-plus months tenure on the platform. Underlying classified data released on Hugging Face ( huggingface.co/datasets/Anthropic/EconomicIndex ) under a permissive license. Pairs with Brynjolfsson, Li, Raymond (ref-22 + ref-40) as the macro-level complement to the 2023 customer-support micro-experiment. Reader file at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/anthropic-economic-index-q1-2026-primitives-learning-curves-ch24.md . Cited in: Ch 24

  52. Anthropic, “Prompt caching with Claude,” product documentation, accessed May 2026, platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-caching (cache reads at 0.10x base input, cache writes at 1.25x base for 5-minute lifetimes and 2x base for 1-hour lifetimes, workspace-level isolation effective February 5, 2026 on Claude API and Azure AI Foundry); Anthropic, “Batch processing,” product documentation, platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/batch-processing (50 percent discount on both input and output tokens for asynchronous workloads with up to 24-hour completion windows, most batches finishing under one hour; stacks with prompt caching for compounded reductions on long-context evaluation pipelines); Anthropic, “Pricing,” platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing (Claude Haiku 4.5 at $1 per million input tokens and $5 per million output tokens; Sonnet 4.5/4.6 at $3 input and $15 output; Opus 4.7 at $5 input and $25 output; batch rates exactly half of standard rates across the tier); Anthropic, “Introducing Claude Haiku 4.5,” October 15, 2025, anthropic.com/news/claude-haiku-4-5 (one-third Sonnet 4 cost, four-to-five-times throughput on representative coding and computer-use tasks, first Haiku model with extended thinking and computer use); Hoff, “Anthropic silently dropped prompt cache TTL from 1 hour to 5 minutes,” dev.to, March 2026, dev.to/whoffagents/anthropic-silently-dropped-prompt-cache-ttl-from-1-hour-to-5-minutes-16ao (default ephemeral cache TTL reverted from 1 hour to 5 minutes on March 6, 2026 without public announcement; workloads with hourly cadence and 10,000-token cached prompts can see 100x cost regression unless TTL is pinned explicitly via "cache_control": {"type": "ephemeral", "ttl": "1h"} ). Wiki synthesis: wiki/external/frontier/ai-anthropic-batch-caching-cost-economics-2026-ch05.md . Cited in: Ch 5
  53. Anthropic, “Prompt caching” API documentation (2026 pricing pages at platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-caching and platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing ); cache-write 1.25x base input on the 5-minute TTL, 2x on the 1-hour TTL; cache-read 0.1x base input (90-percent discount); Batch API stacks a 50-percent discount yielding ~95-percent total reduction on cache-heavy workloads; February-5 2026 transition to workspace-level cache isolation. Lilian Li, “When to Use Prompt Caching: From Cost Economics to Architectural Practice,” Medium, January 2026 (prefix-based invalidation; one-character drift kills the cache). EMARKETER + TransUnion, “US Marketing Mix Modeling Survey,” July 2025: 46.9 percent of U.S. marketers planning to increase MMM investment; demand-side complement to the cost compression. Adweek, “Hershey Bets on Agentic AI to Rethink $2B in Marketing Spend” (late 2025); ALM Corp, “Hershey AI Marketing System: How Mutinex and Tracer Are Changing Media Decisions in Real Time” (2026): Mutinex underpinned by Claude and Gemini, monthly-decision cadence on media and trade spend, twelve cycles per year against the prior triannual schedule. Cited in: Ch 24
  54. Anthropic, “Writing effective tools for AI agents,” 2025 ( anthropic.com/engineering/writing-tools-for-agents ). Cited in: Ch 27
  55. Anthropic. (2024, August 14). Prompt caching with Claude (public beta): up to 90% cost reduction on cached input tokens and 2x latency improvement on Sonnet 3.5 and Haiku 3. Followed September 19, 2024 by the Contextual Retrieval recipe (chunk summary + embeddings + BM25 + reranker), which cuts retrieval failure from 5.7% to 1.9%. anthropic.com/news/prompt-caching ; anthropic.com/news/contextual-retrieval . Cited in: Ch 1
  56. Anthropic. (2024, December 19). Building Effective Agents. Five canonical patterns: prompt chaining, routing, parallelization (sectioning and voting), orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer. anthropic.com/research/building-effective-agents . Anthropic. (2025, June). How we built our multi-agent research system. Production Research feature: lead Opus 4 agent spawns three to five Sonnet 4 workers in parallel; runs use roughly fifteen times the tokens of a single chat turn. anthropic.com/engineering/multi-agent-research-system . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-the-agent-ch01.md and ai-multi-agent-research-architecture-ch04.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  57. Anthropic. (2025, April 21). Values in the Wild. Anonymized analysis of 700,000 Claude.ai conversations from one week in Feb 2025; 308,210 (44%) involved value judgments. Claude strongly supported the user's values 28.2%, reframed 6.6%, resisted strongly 3.0%. anthropic.com/research/values-wild . Cited in: Ch 1
  58. Anthropic. (2025-2026). Claude Sonnet 4.5 / Haiku 4.5 / Opus 4.5-4.7 release arc, September 29, 2025 to April 16, 2026. Sonnet 4.5 (Sep 29, 2025): 30-hour autonomous coding-task ceiling, 61.4 percent on OSWorld against Sonnet 4's 42.2 percent four months earlier, parallel tool execution, ASL-3 deployment. Haiku 4.5 (Oct 15, 2025): $1 / $5 per MTok at near-frontier performance. Opus 4.5 (Nov 24, 2025): 76 percent fewer output tokens at matched Sonnet 4.5 performance, 29 percent gain over Sonnet 4.5 on Vending-Bench, programmatic tool calling, tool search tool, effort parameter. Opus 4.6 (Feb 5, 2026) and Sonnet 4.6 (Feb 17, 2026): adaptive thinking, compaction API, 1M-token context generally available at standard rates on March 13, 2026. Opus 4.7 (Apr 16, 2026): 70 percent on CursorBench against Opus 4.6 at 58 percent, advisor-tool pairing, vision images to 2,576 pixels long-edge. Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 retire June 15, 2026. anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-4-5 ; anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5 ; anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-7 ; platform.claude.com/docs/en/release-notes/overview . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-claude-sonnet-opus-cadence-2025-2026-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1
  59. Anthropic. (2026). Claude API documentation: messages endpoint, context windows, streaming. Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.7 reach 1,000,000 tokens; Haiku 4.5 reaches 200,000. docs.anthropic.com . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-the-file-ch01.md and ai-the-wire-ch01.md. Cited in: Ch 1
  60. Anthropic. (2026, April). Web search tool, generally available. anthropic.com/news/web-search-api . ChatGPT and Gemini shipped equivalents in the same window. Cited in: Ch 1
  61. Anthropic. (Oct 22, 2024 – Apr 16, 2026). Computer use, Claude for Chrome, OSWorld trajectory. Sonnet 3.5 beta launched at OSWorld 14.9% single-shot; Opus 4.7 reaches 78.0% on OSWorld-Verified, the first frontier entry above the 72.4% human-expert baseline. Claude for Chrome expanded to all paid plans Dec 2025 with administrator allowlists; autonomous prompt-injection attack rate 23.6% pre-mitigation, 11.2% post. OpenAI Operator and Google Project Mariner shipped in 2025 and were folded into ChatGPT Agent and Gemini Agent. anthropic.com/news/3-5-models-and-computer-use .   88. Cross-vendor prompt-caching parity, Aug 2024 – May 2025. Anthropic's Aug 14, 2024 launch anchors the steeper end (write 1.25x, read 0.1x). One production write-up cut a monthly bill from $720 to $72 by restructuring the prompt. openai.com/index/api-prompt-caching . Coca-Cola Refresh Your Holidays 2025 sequel by VML, WPP Open X, Silverside AI, and Secret Level (coca-colacompany.com/media-center/coca-cola-refreshes-givers-of-the-season-embraces-ai-powered-storytelling-in-global-holiday-campaign; Mint Mobile MINTernet is Real 2025 ad starring Ryan Reynolds and the human Tilly Norwood, produced by Maximum Effort to launch the $30/month 5G home internet plan (variety.com/2025/digital/news/ryan-reynolds-real-tilly-norwood-not-ai-actress-mint-mobile-1236552857/; Books: John Hegarty, Hegarty on Advertising: Turning Intelligence into Magic (Thames and Hudson, 2011) (thamesandhudson.com/products/hegarty-on-advertising; amazon.com/Hegarty-Advertising-John/dp/0500515565); Hegarty on Creativity: There Are No Rules (Thames and Hudson, 2014); Alex Murrell, "John Hegarty: Hegarty on Creativity" summary (alexmurrell.co.uk/summaries/john-hegarty-hegarty-on-creativity). Levi's 501 Launderette case (Boxing Day 1985, Nick Kamen lead, Marvin Gaye 1968 I Heard It Through the Grapevine needle-drop): Marketing Week, "How Levi's 'Laundrette' ad led to an 800% sales boost" (marketingweek.com/levis-laundrette-sales-boost/); Levi Strauss and Company, "How Levi's Laundrette Ad Reignited the 501" (levistrauss.com/2020/11/19/levis-laundrette-ad-reignited-the-501/); Wikipedia , "Nick Kamen" (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nick_Kamen). Audi Vorsprung durch Technik line (1982 onward, German untranslated in UK advertising, coined in its BBH form by Hegarty in 1984): The Prodcast, "Sir John Hegarty interview" (theprodcast.com/john-hegarty/). Hegarty interviews on the doctrine, creative pragmatism, and the 2026 environment: LBB Online, "Sir John Hegarty: Advertising's Problem Is that Creative People Aren't at the Top" (lbbonline.com/news/Sir-John-Hegarty-Advertisings-Problem-Is-that-Creative-People-Arent-at-the-Top); Marketing Week, "Sir John Hegarty: Creativity is receding from the world of marketing" (marketingweek.com/john-hegarty-great-advertising/); The Drum, "Sir John Hegarty: Advertising needs culture and why consolidation doesn't work" (thedrum.com/news/2018/12/05/sir-john-hegarty-advertising-needs-culture-and-why-consolidation-doesnt-work); WePresent, "Sir John Hegarty's 10 rules for a creative life" (wepresent.wetransfer.com/stories/manifesto-sir-john-hegarty).   128. Mark Ritson, "We know what distinctive marketing looks like. Now let's agree what to call it," The Drum , October 6, 2025 (thedrum.com/news/2025/10/06/mark-ritson-we-know-what-distinctive-marketing-looks-now-let-s-agree-what-call-it); "Did Mark Ritson finally manage to codify distinctive marketing? No, but it is a start," The Drum , October 10, 2025 (thedrum.com/news/2025/10/10/did-mark-ritson-finally-manage-codify-distinctive-marketing-no-it-start); "Ritson's 10-step guide to 'stand on the shoulders' of advertising effectiveness giants," Marketing Week , 2025 (marketingweek.com/ritsons-10-step-advertising-effectiveness/); MiniMBA, "Brand Codes: First, they must know it's me" (minimba.com/brand-codes-first-they-must-know-its-me/). Three findings the 2024 references do not yet carry: four competing terms ("well branded," "brand codes," "distinctive brand assets," "fluent devices") describe the same underlying concept; less than half of ads audiences see are correctly attributed to the brand after the ad ends in the 2025 cross-channel weighted mean; brand recall approaches one hundred percent when seven branded references appear in a thirty-second spot, which converts the System1-Effies seven-codes inflection point (ref-49) into a production-stage cadence rule (one branded reference roughly every four seconds, two-second-or-more continuous appearances counting as one). Course-internal synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ritson-distinctive-marketing-nomenclature-2025-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16

Salesloft Announces Rhythm Powered by Conductor AI , salesloft.com newsroom (the workflow layer ingesting Drift first-party signals and ranking rep actions on the resulting buyer-intent score). Salesloft. (2024, February). Salesloft Acquires Drift , salesloft.com newsroom (the Drift signal feed Conductor AI scores against). Salesloft. (2025-2026). Results Are In: AI-Powered Salesloft Rhythm Drives Meaningful Productivity and Revenue Outcomes for Global Sales Organizations ; December 2025 Release Notes , Salesloft Champions community (Signal-Enhanced Research Briefs combining recent engagement signals into the rep’s pre-call brief). Procurement framing aligned with the four-vendor pricing-unit comparison (Agentforce per seat, Breeze per resolved conversation, Rhythm per signal-acted-on, Gong per call analyzed) elaborated in core-course/wiki/external/frontier/b2b-saas-sales-ai-rollouts-2024-2026-ch23.md . Cited in: Ch 23
  142. Patagonia, Worn Wear FY25 Impact Report , 2025; The Cleanest Line journal archives, patagonia.com/stories ; Patagonia case bible synthesis, internal source. Cited in: Ch 17
  143. Peep Laja, CXL Institute landing-page-optimization training program (formerly ConversionXL), 2014-present, training materials at cxl.com . The PXL framework appears in CXL training video 4.2 and is documented in Laja's annual State of Conversion-Rate-Optimization report, 2024. Cited in: Ch 13
  144. Pink, D. H. (2026, March 12). AI Is Getting Smarter. Humans Need These Skills , danielpink.substack.com/p/6-human-skills-ai-still-cant-replace (six-skill frame layered above the original 2012 ABC: asking better questions, developing good taste, iterating relentlessly, composing pieces into something meaningful, allocating human and machine talent, acting with integrity). Companion: Pink, D. H. (2024, September 24). Selling in the Age of AI and Big Data , Summation podcast (formerly World of DaaS ), worldofdaas.com/p/daniel-pink (forty-nine-minute statement framing the AI era as a continuation of the parity-condition reversal; the human seller’s leverage moves up the funnel from the close to the framing of the problem). Pink, D. H. (2025-2026). AI guide , danpink.com/ai-guide. Johns Hopkins Imagine online course. (2025). Smarter Thinking and Better Living in an AI World: A Conversation with Daniel Pink , imagine.jhu.edu. Pink, D. H. (2012). To Sell Is Human , Riverhead, supplies the original new ABC and the six-pitch toolkit (one-word, question, rhyming, subject-line, Twitter, Pixar) the 2026 frame extends rather than replaces. Replication-crisis caveat on Pink’s 2010-era citations: Senay-Albarracin-Noguchi 2010 interrogative-self-talk had partial-failure replication attempts (Hashim & Andersson, 2014, Discourse Processes ); Galinsky 2006 power-and-perspective E-test included in 2020-2026 social-psychology replicability reviews (Klein & Swann, 2026, Perspectives on Psychological Science , journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/17456916251401849). Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/pink-recent-commentary-2024-2026-ch23.md . Cited in: Ch 23

  154. Sam Altman, “Reflections,” blog.samaltman.com, January 5, 2025, blog.samaltman.com/reflections . Approximately 2,100-word personal essay opening 2025 with three named claims: the verbatim AGI-confidence declaration (“We are now confident we know how to build AGI as we have traditionally understood it.”), the verbatim 2025 agent-arrival projection (“we may see the first AI agents ‘join the workforce’ and materially change the output of companies”), and the verbatim superintelligence pivot (“beginning to turn our aim beyond that, to superintelligence in the true sense of the word”). The post also includes Altman's first long-form public account of the November 2023 OpenAI board firing (verbatim self-characterization “fired by surprise on a video call” in a Las Vegas hotel room; framing “a big failure of governance”; named gratitude callout to Ron Conway and Brian Chesky). Same-day coverage at Time : time.com/7205596/sam-altman-superintelligence-agi . LessWrong cross-post with 8,683 upvotes within the first week: lesswrong.com/posts/T5p9NEAyrHedC2znD/sam-altman-reflections . Marketing-trade reception at the Marketing AI Institute (Paul Roetzer): marketingaiinstitute.com/blog/sam-altman-agi , framing the marketing-team implication as “agent-like systems carrying out strings of tasks” with continued human oversight. The post functions as the philosophical bookend to Three Observations (Feb 9, 2025; ref-31): Reflections names the goal and the timeline, Three Observations names the cost curve; the two together are the OpenAI public position for the calendar year. Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/ai-altman-reflections-2025-ch01.md . Cited in: Ch 1
  155. Sarah Perez, “Spotify says Wrapped 2025 is its biggest yet, with 200M users in its first day,” TechCrunch , Dec 4, 2025 (https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/04/spotify-says-wrapped-2025-is-its-biggest-yet-with-200m-users-in-its-first-day/ 200M engaged users in first 24 hours, 19% YoY lift, 62-hour 2024 comparison; 500M+ social shares in first day, 41% YoY lift); “Spotify Wrapped 2025 attracted over 200m users in first day,” Music Ally , Dec 5, 2025 (https://musically.com/2025/12/05/spotify-wrapped-2025-attracted-over-200m-users-in-first-day/ Meltwater, “How Spotify Wrapped 2025 Went Viral: Listening Age Insights and Global Trends” (https://www.meltwater.com/en/blog/spotify-wrapped-listening-age-analysis 13.6M global mentions in first month, 2.7M peak Dec 4, 35.1% positive / 17.2% negative sentiment); Spotify Newsroom, “2025 Wrapped” (https://newsroom.spotify.com/2025-wrapped/ Listening Age + Wrapped Party multiplayer features driving sharability lift). The Dec 3 launch is a calendar-locked annual cue, the artifact carries the brand identity inside it, and the audience does the publication: a platform-level §19.3 Triggers cue-pairing case at the share-card grain. Synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/spotify-wrapped-2025-ch19.md (full operational decomposition of calendar discipline + share-card composition + publication mechanic + brand-partner extension; documents the 2024 AI-podcast flop as falsifying counter-example with the 62-hour-vs-24-hour time-to-200M milestone gap and the 14-vs-60-percent day-two attenuation gap; the 2027 refresh anchor for Wrapped-grade earned-media cases). Cited in: Ch 19
  156. Schluntz and Zhang, “Building Effective Agents,” Anthropic engineering blog, December 19, 2024 ( anthropic.com/engineering/building-effective-agents ); companion implementations at github.com/anthropics/anthropic-cookbook/tree/main/patterns/agents ; the workflows-vs-agents architectural distinction (workflows orchestrated through predefined code paths versus agents that dynamically direct their own tool usage) and the five-pattern workflow taxonomy (prompt chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-workers, evaluator-optimizer), now the canonical reference cited across the 2025-2026 agentic-analytics literature; Hershey-Mutinex classified as orchestrator-workers with a human-analyst orchestrator and Claude-or-Gemini specialist workers, MaRGen (ref-44) as prompt chaining, Recast multi-stage MMM plus GeoLift (ref-48) as parallelization, and the Evidently AI validation layer (ref-49) as evaluator-optimizer. DataForSEO TypeScript MCP server github.com/dataforseo/mcp-server-typescript covering SERP, KEYWORDS_DATA, ONPAGE, and DATAFORSEO_LABS modules; DataForSEO SERP API pricing dataforseo.com/apis/serp-api/pricing (Standard $0.0006/query, Priority $0.0012/query, Live $0.002/query, $50 minimum deposit, pay-as-you-go). Microsoft, Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise pricing , microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365-copilot/pricing/enterprise ; eesel AI, 2026 Copilot pricing analysis , eesel.ai/blog/copilot-pricing ; EPC Group, Microsoft 365 Copilot Pricing & Licensing Enterprise Guide 2026 , epcgroup.net (Q4 2025 bundling decision: Copilot for Sales repositioned from $50-per-user-per-month standalone to zero-incremental-cost feature inside the $30 base license; ~90M eligible enterprise seats). Microsoft 365 Copilot paid-seat counts: creati.ai (February 2026, ~15M paid seats with 160% YoY growth) and windowsnews.ai (April 2026, ~20M paid seats). Salesforce, Agentforce 2.0 launch , salesforce.com/news/stories/agentforce-2-launch , December 2024; Agentforce 360 launch , salesforce.com/news/stories/agentforce-360-launch (the parallel Salesforce-side platform commit). Third-party comparative analysis: 1Point1, Agentforce vs Microsoft Copilot for Sales , 1point1.com ; Smartbridge, Copilot Studio vs Salesforce Agentforce: Two Paths to Enterprise Agentic AI , smartbridge.com . Synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/microsoft-copilot-envelopment-standalone-sales-ai-2025-2027-ch23.md . Cited in: Ch 23
  176. WARC, System1, Prophet, Bera.ai, and Analytic Partners, The Multiplier Effect: A CMO's Guide to Brand-Building in the Performance Era , June 2025 (warc.com/the-multiplier-effect; system1group.com/the-multiplier-effect; prophet.com/2025/01/the-multiplier-effect-a-cmos-guide-to-brand-building-in-the-performance-era/). Andrew Tindall (System1) and co-authors fuse the System1 Test Your Ad database (~150,000 ads on the 1.0-5.9 Star Rating, calibrated against year-on-year market-share gain) with the North American Effie Awards effectiveness archive across approximately 1,200 paired ads to identify the multiplicative chain of creative quality, distinctive-asset density, and run-time as the levers behind a roughly 12x top-quartile-to-bottom-quartile profit multiplier; temporal sub-result of ~5x at six months and ~7.5x at three years against a median-creative baseline; ~7 brand codes as the empirical inflection point at which recall approaches saturation; 92.1 percent of strong equity-building ads also winning short-term. Mark Ritson called the result "the most important advertising thinking in 10 years" in his June 24, 2025 Mi3 column (mi-3.com.au/24-06-2025/most-important-advertising-thinking-10-years-ritson-says-system1-effies-ad-effectiveness; minimba.com/mark-ritson-data-finally-shows-us-the-three-factors-of-good-creative). Course-internal synthesis: core-course/wiki/external/frontier/system1-effies-multiplier-2025-ch16.md . Cited in: Ch 16
  177. Weng, L., “LLM Powered Autonomous Agents,” Lil’Log, June 23, 2023 ( lilianweng.github.io/posts/2023-06-23-agent ); “Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning,” November 28, 2024 ( lilianweng.github.io/posts/2024-11-28-reward-hacking ); “Why We Think,” May 1, 2025, written at Thinking Machines Lab ( lilianweng.github.io/posts/2025-05-01-thinking ). Four-component framework (Planning / Memory / Tool Use / Action) with the LLM as brain; planning sub-areas (Chain of Thought, Tree of Thoughts, ReAct, Reflexion, Chain of Hindsight, Algorithm Distillation); memory tiers (sensory / short-term / long-term via MIPS retrieval — LSH, ANNOY, HNSW, FAISS, ScaNN). Twitter distillation same day: “Agent = LLM + memory + planning skills + tool use.” The 2024 reward-hacking post warns that any RL-style optimization on an agent loop will exploit the eval (canonical example: “the model learns to modify unit tests to pass coding tasks”). The 2025 “Why We Think” post reframes extended thinking as variable-compute allocated to sub-task hardness (Best-of-N, beam-with-PRM, self-consistency, SCoRe, DeepSeek-R1 RL-for-reasoning). Toolkit synthesis at core-course/wiki/external/frontier/lilian-weng-llm-agent-taxonomy-2023-2025-ch27.md . Cited in: Ch 27
  178. 5WPR Citation Source Index 2026 (680M citations Aug 2024-Apr 2026; top-15-domain concentration 68%); per-platform data via Profound, "AI Platform Citation Patterns," tryprofound.com . Cited in: Ch 18
  179. Xu, Hu, Das, Wang, “Causal Machine Learning for Promotions: Industry Evidence and Applications,” 3rd Workshop on Causal Inference and Machine Learning in Practice, KDD 2025, Toronto, August 4, 2025 ( causal-machine-learning.github.io/kdd2025-workshop/papers/16.pdf ); DoorDash production deployment with Double Machine Learning (Chernozhukov et al., The Econometrics Journal 21(1), 2018, C1-C68) as the winning architecture against S-Learner, T-Learner, and an internal Category Transformer baseline; offline Qini 8.03 percent and Uplift-at-30 of 1.11 percentage points out-of-sample; online A/B test producing 2.44 percent versus 2.43 percent order-rate lift at cost index 55 versus 100 (binary treatment) and 2.56 percent lift at cost index 28 (continuous discount-depth treatment). Open-source production tooling at github.com/py-why/EconML (Microsoft Research ALICE project) and github.com/uber/causalml (Uber Engineering); Qini curve methodology at insightful-data-lab.com/2025/08/23/qini-curve/ ; Künzel, Sekhon, Bickel, Yu, “Metalearners for Estimating Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Using Machine Learning,” PNAS 116(10), 2019, 4156-4165 (S-Learner / T-Learner / X-Learner taxonomy); California Management Review, “To Treat or Not To Treat? Five Lessons Learned from Using Uplift Modeling to Optimize Marketing Campaigns,” November 2025 ( cmr.berkeley.edu/2025/11/to-treat-or-not-to-treat-five-lessons-learned-from-using-uplift-modeling-to-optimize-marketing-campaigns/ ); DoorDash Engineering Blog, “Smarter Promotions with Causal Machine Learning,” 2025 ( careersatdoordash.com/blog/doordash-smarter-promotions-with-causal-machine-learning/ ). Cited in: Ch 24
